member.summary:
Contains the path of the current description file
The description file path is used to locate the description files as they are required while retrieving the journal records, and should be set before calling the GetJournal method. The GetJournal method can update the value of this property.
If the value of this property is null (""), the journal path returned by the GetJournalPath method call is used as the default value to locate any description files.

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin
member.summary: The DatabaseAdmin class encapsulates the behaviour required to write standalone or integrated database administration tools for your Joob applications; for example, for runtime Joob systems or for online database backups controlled from your user applications.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.AbortBackup
member.summary:
The AbortBackup method of the DatabaseAdmin class terminates a backup transaction and cancels pending backup operations.
Although the AbortBackup method does not remove any files that have been copied, the backup will not be usable.
File backups that are in progress can be interrupted only if you have enabled progress event notifications. (For details, see the EnableProgressEvents method.) If backup progress events are disabled (the default), file backups that are in progress continue until files have been copied. If the database is in a quiescent read-only mode, the AbortBackup method ends this mode, permitting updating transactions to be processed.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.BackupAllDatabaseFiles(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BackupAllDatabaseFiles method of the DatabaseAdmin class initiates a backup of all physical database files, optionally excluding system files, to the directory specified in the backupDir parameter. (The backup directory must be a valid directory that is relative to the server.) For the majority of database backups, it is not necessary to back up system files, as they are not updated in development or runtime systems and can be shared by multiple JOOB environments (by using the SystemFileDirectory parameter in the [PersistentDb] section of the JOOB initialization file). System files are updated only when the RootSchema or JOOB patch files are loaded by using the Schema Load utility. Set the verifyFiles parameter to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified. In a checked file backup, objects are read using the database access routines and object caching mechanisms, and at the same time, a verification of the data and indexes is performed. The verification performs various consistency checks similar to a database certify, to ensure the integrity of the backup. Furthermore, additional checksum information is added to the backup, to allow restore operations to verify the integrity of the backup as the data is restored. Set the compressFiles parameter to true if you want to compress data on the fly as it is backed up. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup. When both the verifyFiles and compressFiles parameters are set to false, a fast file backup is performed. In a fast file backup, database files are backed up in a similar fashion to a standard file copy, using large buffers and asynchronous I/O to speed up the copy process. The fast backup mode bypasses the database access routines and cache management, and does not verify data as it is backed up. Set the overwriteFiles parameter to true if you want to allow file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing file is detected. Use the quiesce parameter to allow a quiesced read-only backup transaction to be specified. When you set this parameter to true, the database is placed in a quiescent state by first allowing current active transactions to be completed, flushing modified buffers for cache to the stable database. In this mode, physical database files contain all committed updates to the database, and the files are opened in read-only mode with shared read access allowing external backup process to safely copy database files. In the quiescent mode, updating transactions are not permitted and attempts to execute database transactions raise a database exception. When a backup is performed in the quiescent mode, the physical database files are guaranteed to contain all database updates and a quiesced backup does not require backup recovery. Set the quiesce parameter to false to allow updates during the backup process. When restoring a database backed up fully online (that is, this parameter is set to false), the restoration process requires the recovery of backed up transaction journals. A backup recovery is required after restoring files that were fully backed up online (that is, the quiesce parameter was set to false). The droppedFiles parameter specifies an array of the database files that are not backed up.
member.param: The backup directory must be a valid directory that is relative to the server.
Set to false if you do not want to include system files in your backup process (that is, files with kind of DatabaseFileKind.System).
Set to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified.
Set to true if you want to compress data on the fly as it is backed up.
Set to true if you want to allow file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory.
When set to true, the database is placed in a quiescent state by first allowing current active transactions to be completed, flushing modified buffers for cache to the stable database.
member.returns: An array of the database files that are not backed up.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.BackupDatabaseFiles(System.String,JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFileKind,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BackupDatabaseFiles method of the DatabaseAdmin class backs up the kinds (categories) of files specified in the fileKinds parameter to the directory specified in the backupDir parameter. The backup directory specified in the backupDir parameter must be a valid directory that is relative to the server. Use the fileKinds parameter to select files for backup by their kind, or enumeration group. You can select multiple file kinds in a single call, by summing the DatabaseFileKind enumeration values. For example, to select user schema files, environmental files, and user data files, you could pass the following value for the fileKinds parameter: DatabaseFileKind.Environmental + DatabaseFileKind.User_Schema + DatabaseFileKind.User_Data Set the verifyFiles parameter to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified. In a checked file backup, objects are read using the database access routines and object caching mechanisms, and at the same time, a verification of the data and indexes is performed. The verification performs various consistency checks similar to a database certify, to ensure the integrity of the backup. Furthermore, additional checksum information is added to the backup, to allow restore operations to verify the integrity of the backup as the data is restored. Set the compressFiles parameter to true if you want to compress backed up data. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup. When both the verifyFiles and compressFiles parameters are set to false, a fast file backup is performed. In a fast file backup, database files are backed up in a similar fashion to a standard file copy, using large buffers and asynchronous I/O to speed up the copy process. The fast backup mode bypasses the database access-routines and cache management, and does not verify data as it is backed up. Set the overwriteFiles parameter to true if you want file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing file is detected. Use the quiesce parameter to allow a quiesced read-only backup transaction to be specified. When you set this parameter to true, the database is placed in a quiescent state by first allowing current active transactions to be completed, flushing modified buffers for cache to the stable database. In this mode, physical database files contain all committed updates to the database, and the files are opened in read-only mode with shared read access allowing external backup process to safely copy database files. In the quiescent mode, updating transactions are not permitted and attempts to execute database transactions raise a database exception. When a backup is performed in the quiescent mode, the physical database files are guaranteed to contain all database updates and a quiesced backup does not require backup recovery. Set the quiesce parameter to false to allow updates during the backup process. When restoring a database backed up fully online (that is, this parameter is set to false), the restoration process requires the recovery of backed up transaction journals. A backup recovery is required after restoring files that were fully backed up online (that is, the quiesce parameter was set to false). The droppedFiles parameter specifies an array of the database files that are not backed up.
member.param: The backup directory must be a valid directory that is relative to the server.
DatabaseFileKind enumeration values that can be combined select what types of files to backup
Set to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified.
Set to true if you want to compress data on the fly as it is backed up.
Set to true if you want to allow file backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory.
When set to true, the database is placed in a quiescent state by first allowing current active transactions to be completed, flushing modified buffers for cache to the stable database.
member.returns: An array of the database files that are not backed up.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.BackupJournal(System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BackupJournal method of the DatabaseAdmin class backs up the transaction journal identified by the number parameter from the journal directory specified in the sourceDir parameter to the directory specified in the backupDir parameter. (The backup directory must be a valid directory relative to the server.) If the value of the sourceDir parameter is null, the current journal directory is used. Set the verify parameter to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified. In a checked file backup, objects are read using the database access routines and object caching mechanisms, and at the same time, a verification of the data and indexes is performed. The verification performs various consistency checks similar to a database certify, to ensure the integrity of the backup. Furthermore, additional checksum information is added to the backup, to allow restore operations to verify the integrity of the backup as the data is restored. Set the compress parameter to true if you want to compress backed up data. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup. When both the verify and compress parameters are set to false, a fast file backup is performed. In a fast file backup, database files are backed up in a similar fashion to a standard file copy, using large buffers and asynchronous I/O to speed up the copy process. The fast backup mode bypasses the database access-routines and cache management, and does not verify data as it is backed up. Set the overwriteDest parameter to true if you want transaction journal backups to overwrite existing journals in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing transaction journal is detected.
member.param: Transaction journal identified by this parameter from the journal directory
The journal directory
The backup directory must be a valid directory that is relative to the server.
Set to true if you want the backed up file checked, or verified.
Set to true if you want to compress data on the fly as it is backed up.
Set to true if you want transaction journal backups to overwrite existing journals in the destination backup directory

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.BeginBackup(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BeginBackup method of the DatabaseAdmin class signals the start of a database backup transaction in which multiple database files are copied. The backupDir parameter specifies a default location for file backups and the directory where control files are copied when the backup is committed. (The backup directory must be a valid directory that is relative to the server.) Use the quiesced parameter to specify whether updates are allowed during the backup process (by setting this parameter to false) or if the backup is to be performed in read-only mode. When restoring a database backed up fully online (that is, this parameter is set to false and the database was available for both read and write access at the time of the backup), the restoration process requires the recovery of backed up transaction journals. When a database is backed up in the quiescent mode, the physical database files are guaranteed to contain all database updates, and a recovery is not required when the backed up database is restored. Set the quiesced parameter to true to specify that the database is locked for write access during the backup operation and to enable archival recovery. (Database records can still be read in this mode, although not updated.) When a database is backed up in a quiescent mode, any threads that attempt to start a new transaction are first blocked, waiting for pending transactions to complete. All dirty buffers for objects resident in the file are flushed and the file is locked against further updates. At this point, threads blocked by the BeginTransaction instruction are allowed to continue. (Database operations that attempt to update a file in read-only mode receive an exception.)
member.param: Specifies the location for file backups and the directory where control files are copied when the backup is committed
Set to true to allow updates to occur during the backup process

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.ChangeDbAccessMode(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseMode,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseUsage)
member.summary: The ChangeDbAccessMode method of the DatabaseAdmin class changes the access mode of the database.
member.param: Use the mode parameter to specify the database access mode that you require
Use the usage parameter to specify the database usage that you require.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.CloseCurrentJournal
member.summary: The CloseCurrentJournal method of the DatabaseAdmin class closes the active transaction journal and switches to a new transaction journal.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.CommitBackup
member.summary: The CommitBackup method of the DatabaseAdmin class signals the successful completion of a database backup transaction in which multiple database files have been copied. This method triggers the copying of the database control file (and if archival recovery is disabled, the current transaction journal) to the default backup directory specified in the BeginBackup method. If the database is in a quiescent read-only mode, the CommitBackup method ends this mode, permitting updating transactions to be processed.
member.remarks: Note The CommitBackup method also marks an online database backup as valid. If this has not completed, the backup cannot be used.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.DisableByteProgressEvents
member.summary: The DisableByteProgressEvents method of the DatabaseAdmin class disables the notification of operation and progress events reporting the number of bytes of a file that have been backed up. Operation and progress events are disabled by default.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.DisableProgressEvents
member.summary: The DisableProgressEvents method of the DatabaseAdmin class disables the notification of operation and progress events reporting the percentage of a file that has been backed up. Operation and progress events are disabled by default.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.EnableByteProgressEvents(System.Int32)
member.summary: The EnableByteProgressEvents method of the DatabaseAdmin class enables the notification of operation and progress events for file backups at the progress interval specified in the increment parameter, which represents a number of bytes. A value of zero (0) for the increment parameter specifies the lowest allowed value of 128K bytes. When operation and progress event notifications are enabled: 􀂄 A progress event is notified by each DatabaseFile instance whenever the specified number of bytes of the file has been copied. 􀂄 An operation event is notified by each DatabaseFile instance of the backup operation being performed on the file.
member.param: Progress interval specified in a number of bytes
member.remarks: Note Enabling operation and progress notification is likely to have some impact on the elapsed time of file backups.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.DoCheckpoint
member.summary:
The DoCheckpoint method of the DatabaseAdmin class causes a database checkpoint operation to be queued to the database worker thread.
This method is intended for use by backup algorithms and mechanisms that need knowledge of journal activity and recovery restart points and which require the ability to cause recovery restart point re-evaluation. See also the GetLastCheckpoint method in the following section.
member.remarks: Caution The processing burden injected by the initiation of a checkpoint is not easily determined, as system activity is a major factor; however, there is always a cost to performance, as I/O must be performed. Inappropriate use of the doCheckpoint method could cause significant performance degradation.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.EnableProgressEvents(System.Int32)
member.summary: The EnableProgressEvents method of the DatabaseAdmin class enables the notification of operation and progress events for file backups at the progress interval specified in the increment parameter, which represents a percentage of the size of the file. When operation and progress event notifications are enabled: 􀂄 A progress event is notified by each DatabaseFile instance whenever the specified percentage increment of the file has been copied. 􀂄 An operation event is notified by each DatabaseFile instance of the backup operation being performed on the file.
member.remarks: Note Enabling operation and progress notification is likely to have some impact on the elapsed time of file backups.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetAbortJournalNumber
member.summary:
The GetAbortJournalNumber method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the number of the journal containing the Begin Transaction record of the oldest active transaction.
When this method is called on a secondary database, it returns the oldest journal required for an undo operation in the event of a hostile takeover or the next required replay journal if no journals have been replayed in the current session.
If there are no active transactions, the number of the oldest journal required for recovery is returned.
member.returns: The oldest journal required for an undo operation

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetAllDatabaseFiles
member.summary: The GetAllDatabaseFiles method of the DatabaseAdmin class populates a DatabaseFile array with references to all database files found by searching all schemas from the RootSchema down through the schema hierarchy.
member.returns: Array of references to all database files found by searching all schemas from the RootSchema down through the schema hierarchy

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetCreationTimestamp
member.summary: The GetCreationTimestamp method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns a DateTime containing the date and time the database was created.
member.returns: The date and time the database was created

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetCurrentJournalDirectory
member.summary: The GetCurrentJournalDirectory method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the name of the current directory for transaction journals.
member.returns: Name of the current directory for transaction journals

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetCurrentJournalName
member.summary: The GetCurrentJournalName method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the name of the current active transaction journal.
member.returns: Name of the current active transaction journal

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetCurrentJournalNumber
member.summary: The GetCurrentJournalNumber method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the number of the current active transaction journal. When this method is called on a secondary database, it returns the last replayed journal number or the next required replay journal if no journals have been replayed in the current session.
member.returns: The number of the current active transaction journal, or last replayed journal number on a SDS secondary

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetDatabaseFiles(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFileKind)
member.summary: The GetDatabaseFiles method of the DatabaseAdmin class populates a DatabaseFile array with references to database files of the kinds specified in the parameter, by searching all schemas from the Root Schema down through the schema hierarchy. Use the parameter to select files for backup by their kind, or category group. You can select multiple file kinds in a single call, by summing the kind constant values. For example, to select user schema files, environmental files, and user data files, you could pass the following value for the parameter: DatabaseFileKind.Environmental + DatabaseFileKind.User_Schema + DatabaseFileKind.User_Data
member.param: Array with references to selected database files
member.returns: Select files for backup by their kind

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetLastCheckpoint(System.Int32@,System.UInt32@)
member.summary:
The GetLastCheckpoint method of the DatabaseAdmin class retrieves the journal number and byte offset of the last database checkpoint.
This method is intended for use by backup algorithms and mechanisms that need knowledge of journal activity and recovery restart points and require the ability to cause recovery restart point re-evaluation. See also the DoCheckpoint method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetLatestBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The GetLatestBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the date and time the database was last backed up without error.
member.returns: The date and time the database was last backed up without error

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetLatestFullBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The GetLatestFullBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the date and time the database was last backed up without error and the backup included all database files; that is, the backup was a full backup.
member.returns: The date and time the database was last backed up without error and the backup included all database files

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetOpenTimestamp
member.summary: The GetOpenTimestamp method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns the most recent date and time the database was opened.
member.returns: The most recent date and time the database was opened

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.GetReasonTrackingStoppedString(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.SDSStopTrackingReason)
member.summary: Returns a string, which is based on a member of the enumeration, describing why tracking was stopped
member.remarks: Valid for both Primary or Secondary
member.param: Enumeration covering reasons why tracking may have stopped

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.IsArchival
member.summary: The IsArchival method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns true if database archival recovery is enabled for the database server node on which the JOOB system is running.
member.returns: True if database archival recovery is enabled

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsAuditStopTracking(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.SDSStopTrackingCodes,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.SDSStopTrackingReason,System.Int32@,System.UInt32@)
member.summary: The SdsAuditStopTracking method of the DatabaseAdmin class specifies a system event number that is returned in the userInfo parameter of your user notification method when an SDSTracking.Stopped event occurs because of a programmatic, SDS Administration utility action or tracking is halted because of an error, to notify subscribers that tracking has been disabled. When this method is invoked on the primary, it causes a Stop Tracking audit record to be written to the current journal. When this record is replayed on an SDS secondary, tracking halts at that point in the audit trail. The value of the scope parameter determines the type of secondary databases that actions the stop tracking command, and is represented by the SDSStopTrackingCodes enumeration. The reason parameter determines the reason tracking was disabled, and is represented by the SDSStopTrackingCodes category global constants listed in the following table. This value is audited and passed to subscribers to the SDSTracking.Stopped system event in the userInfo parameter of the associated userNotify callback method. The SDSReason.Takeover value indicates that tracking stopped during a takeover operation and the SDSReason.RpsReorgHalt value indicates that tracking stopped at transition. The SDSReason.ErrorHalt value indicates that tracking halted due to an error condition (the error code is saved in the SDSSecondary or SDSSecondaryProxy dynamic lastErrorCode attribute). The journal and offset output parameters contain the journal number and byte offset within the journal of the Stop Tracking audit record. These two values together comprise a Log Sequence Number (LSN). When tracking is restarted on the secondary, it resumes at the next audit record.
member.param: The type of secondary databases that actions the stop tracking command
The reason tracking was disabled
Journal number of the journal of the Stop Tracking audit record
The byte offset within the journal of the Stop Tracking audit record

remarks.para: Notes Calling this method neither forces a quiet point nor closes the current journal. The main purpose for this in an RPS context is to establish a journal trigger that coincides with a point-in-time on the primary database, to enable establishing a snapshot of the mapped extent in the target database frozen at that time.
Valid for both Primary or Secondary

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsDisablePrimaryConnection
member.summary: The SdsDisablePrimaryConnection method of the DatabaseAdmin class causes the secondary server to close the connection to its primary server, if open, leaving the connection closed. This places a secondary database in an offline mode in which it can continue tracking and providing read-only database access while not receiving further journals from the primary database. Use the SdsReconnectNow method to attempt to re-establish a connection to the primary. You can also use the SdsDisablePrimaryConnection method to clear and re-establish a disrupted network link. In the event that a connection becomes disrupted because of a problem in the network path, call the sdsDisablePrimaryConnection method to drop the failed connection and then the SdsReconnectNow method to attempt to establish a fresh network connection.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsDisablePrimaryConnectionAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsDisablePrimaryConnectionAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, causes the secondary server specified in the secondary secondaryName parameter to close the connection to its primary server, if open, leaving the connection closed. This places a secondary database in an offline mode in which it can continue tracking and providing read-only database access while not receiving further journals from the primary database.
member.param: Specified secondary server

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsDisableReadAccess
member.summary: The SdsDisableReadAccess method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only if called from a secondary database system, disables read access to persistent objects in the database. When read access is disabled, inquiry applications are not allowed access to persistent objects resident in the database. Any attempt by a user application to access persistent objects when read access is disabled raises an exception. If successful, the value of the ReadAccessDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file is updated to true on the current secondary database server so that the setting is preserved when the server restarts.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsDisableReadAccessAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsDisableReadAccessAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, disables read access to the secondary database that has the secondaryName parameter value in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file matching the name specified in the secondaryName parameter. When read access is disabled on the secondary database, inquiry applications cannot access persistent objects resident in the database server. Any attempt by a user application to access persistent objects when read access is disabled raises an exception. If successful, the value of the ReadAccessDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file on the specified secondary database server is updated to true so that the setting is preserved when the server restarts.
member.param: secondary database name

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsEnableReadAccess
member.summary: The SdsEnableReadAccess method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only if called from a secondary database system, requests the granting of read access to persistent objects in the database. Read access is not granted immediately when interrupted transactions remain pending after a restart operation. Read access is granted only when all remaining interrupted transactions complete. Use the SdsGetTransactions method to determine the status of active (that is, incomplete) transactions. If successful, the value of the ReadAccessDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file is updated to false on the current secondary database server so that the setting is preserved when the server restarts.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsEnableReadAccessAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsEnableReadAccessAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only from a primary database system, requests the granting of read access to persistent objects in the secondary database specified in the secondaryName parameter. Read access is not granted immediately when interrupted transactions remain pending after a restart operation. Read access is granted only when all remaining interrupted transactions complete. Use the SdsGetTransactionsAt method to determine the status of active (that is, incomplete) transactions on the secondary database server. If successful, the value of the ReadAccessDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file is updated to false on the current secondary database server so that the setting is preserved when the server restarts.
member.param: Secondary database name

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetDatabaseRole
member.summary: The SdsGetDatabaseRole method of the DatabaseAdmin class returns an integer value that represents the database role of the server on which the method is executed.

remarks.para: Tip: Use the method on the class to obtain the current database role for the JOOB system in which it is executing without having to create and then delete an instance of this class.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetMyServerInfo
member.summary:
The SdsGetMyServerInfo method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid at the primary and secondary databases nodes, populates a JoobDynamicObject instance describing the SDS attributes of the JOOB system. The caller is responsible for the creation and deletion of the input dynamic object parameter.
When this method is invoked from the primary database system, the input parameter is converted into an SDSPrimary dynamic object, whose name attribute has a value of SDSPrimary and type attribute has a value of SDS_PrimaryType (1). The primary dynamic attributes are listed in the following table.
All audit timestamps are UTC values, which are converted to local time for dynamic attributes. The timestamps of both the primary and secondary are both derived from the UTC audit timestamp recorded by the primary and converted to local time on the secondary (catering for primary and secondary time zones that differ).
When this method is invoked from a secondary database system, the input parameter is converted into an SDSSecondary dynamic object, whose name attribute has a value of SDSsecondary and type attribute has a value of SDS_SecondaryType (3).
member.returns:
Describes the SDS attributes of the JOOB system. The following table describes the properties in the JoobDynamicObject.
Name (JOML Type)Description
connectedSecondaryServers (Integer)Number of secondary servers connected to the primary
currentJournalNumber (Integer)Number of the current write journal
currentJournalTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the current journal converted to a local value
currentJournalTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the current journal as a UTC value
latestCommittedTimestamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the latest commit audit record appended to the audit trail converted to a local value
latestCommittedTimestampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the latest commit audit record appended to the audit trail as a UTC value
maxCommittedTranID (Decimal)Highest transaction id committed by the primary database, which may not be the latest transaction committed
myHostName (String)Computer name of the primary host
myName (String)Name of the primary, specified in the MyName parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetSecondaryInfo(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsGetSecondaryInfo method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, retrieves the attributes of the secondary system specified in the secondaryName parameter, converts the serverInfo input parameter into an SDSSecondary dynamic object, and populates the JoobDynamicObject instance with the attributes and values retrieved from the secondary server. The caller is responsible for creation and deletion of the secondary dynamic object parameter. As it is not valid to execute this method on a secondary database, use the SdsGetMyServerInfo method instead.
member.param: Secondary database name
member.returns:
Attributes and values retrieved from the secondary server
Name (JOML Type)Description
activeTransactions (Integer)Number of active transactions
connectionCheckInterval (Integer)Number of seconds at which the secondary database polls the primary to determine accessibility via the communication paths, specified in the ConnectionPollInterval parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
connectionState (Integer)State of the connection to the primary (for possible values, see enumeration)
currentReplayJournalNumber (Integer)Number of the journal currently replaying
currentReplayJournalTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the journal currently replaying converted to a local value
currentReplayJournalTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the journal currently replaying as a UTC value
latestReadyJournalNumber (Integer)Number of the latest journal that is ready to be replayed
latestReadyJournalTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the latest journal that is ready to be replayed converted to a local value
latestReadyJournalTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the latest journal that is ready to be replayed as a UTC value
latestReplayedAuditTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the of the last audit record replayed by the database tracker converted to a local valuePresent when synceMode is block write
latestReplayedAuditTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the of the last audit record replayed by the database tracker as a UTC valuePresent when synceMode is block write
latestStableAuditTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the of the last audit record written to disk in block write mode converted to a local valuePresent when synceMode is block write
latestStableAuditTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the of the last audit record written to disk in block write mode as a UTC valuePresent when synceMode is block write
myHostName (String)Computer name of the secondary host
myName (String)Name of the secondary, specified in the MyName parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
maxCommittedTranID (Decimal)Transaction id of the maximum committed audit recordPresent when synceMode is block write
maxReplayedTranID (Decimal)Transaction id of the maximum replayed audit recordPresent when synceMode is block write
maxStableTranID (Decimal)Transaction id of the maximum stable audit recordPresent when synceMode is block write
nextReplayJournalNumber (Integer)Number of the next journal to replay
nextReplayJournalTimeStamp (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the next journal to replay converted to a local value
nextReplayJournalTimeStampUTC (TimeStamp)Timestamp of the next journal to replay as a UTC value
primaryHostName (String)Computer name of the primary host
primaryServerName (String)Name of the primary, specified in the PrimaryServerName parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
readAccessDisabled (Boolean)Specifies whether read access is disabled (the value of the ReadAccessDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file)
readAccessGranted (Boolean)Specifies whether read access has been granted
reasonTrackingStopped (Integer)Reason tracking stopped (for possible values, see enumeration)
reconnectInterval (Integer)Frequency (in seconds) at which a secondary database server attempts to reconnect to its primary server when a connection fails or the secondary database loses its connection to the primary database (the value of the ReconnectInterval parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file)
recoveryRequired (Boolean)Specifies whether recovery is required
When a secondary is restarted in a interrupted mode, the recoveryRequired attribute is set, but counts of active and interrupted transaction are not valid until the first journal has been replayed. The recoveryRequired attribute is reset when outstanding interrupted transactions complete.
reorgStatus (Integer)Reorganization status (for possible values, see enumeration)
state (Integer)State of the secondary in relation to the primary (For possible values, see enumeration)
syncMode (Integer)Mode of journal synchronization, specified in the SyncMode parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file (for possible values, see enumeration)
With BlockWrite (2), audit blocks are transferred to the secondary when they are written to the journal on the primary.
With JournalSwitch (1), transfer is delayed until a journal is complete.
tracking (Boolean)Contains true when tracking is active or it contains false if tracking is stopped for any reason
trackingDisabled (Boolean)Specifies whether database tracking (journal replay) process is disabled (the value of the TrackingDisabled parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file)

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetSecondaryProxies
member.summary: The SdsGetSecondaryProxies method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, creates and populates an SDSSecondaryProxy object for each secondary system node that is currently registered on the executing primary system and returns these secondary proxy dynamic objects in the proxies parameter. The SDSSecondaryProxy dynamic object has a JoobDynamicObject class name attribute value of SDSsecondaryProxy and type attribute value of SDS_SecondaryProxyType (2).
member.returns: An array of JoobDynamicObjects, (See for a description of what information the JoobDynamicObject contains)

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetSecondaryProxy(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsGetSecondaryProxy method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, creates and populates an SDSSecondaryProxy object for the secondary system node specified in the secondaryName parameter and returns the secondary proxy dynamic object in the proxyInfo parameter. The SDSSecondaryProxy object returned by the sdsGetSecondaryProxy method contains a subset of the SDSSecondary dynamic object attributes, including nextJournalNumber. The secondary proxy object: 􀂄 Enables you to obtain summary information about a secondary without having to go to the secondary. 􀂄 Can be called when the secondary is disconnected. Note You should call this method to first get a secondary proxy object and check the connectionState attribute value before calling the sdsGetSecondaryInfo method. The SDSSecondaryProxy dynamic object has a JoobDynamicObject class name attribute value of SDSsecondaryProxy and type attribute value of SDS_SecondaryProxyType (2).
member.param: Secondary database name
member.returns:
The secondary proxy dynamic object
Name (JOML Type)Description
connectionCheckInterval (Integer)Number of seconds at which the secondary database polls the primary to determine reachability via the communication paths, specified in the ConnectionPollInterval parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
connectionState (Integer)State of the connection to the primary
hostName (String)Computer name of the secondary proxy host on the primary
lastErrorCode (Integer)Number of the last error that occurred
myName (String)Name of the secondary proxy on the primary, specified in the MyName parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
nextJournalNumber (Integer)Next journal the primary sends if the secondary is catching up or the next write journal when the secondary is mirroring writes from the current journal. It remains valid when the secondary is disconnected.
primaryServerName (String)Name of the primary, specified in the PrimaryServerName parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file
syncMode (Integer)Mode of journal synchronization, specified in the SyncMode parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file (for possible values, see enumeration)
totalSends (Integer64)Count of messages sent to the secondary
totalBlocksSent (Integer64)Count of journal blocks sent to the secondary (there are up to 16 blocks per message)
totalBytesSent (Integer64)Count of bytes sent to the secondary; that is, the total size of all messages sent
totalUncompressedBytes (Integer64)Count of bytes sent to the secondary if compress was disabled

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetTransactions
member.summary: The SdsGetTransactions method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at secondary database systems, creates and populates an SDSTransaction dynamic object for each active transaction that is currently being replayed or isolated on the executing secondary system and returns these transaction dynamic objects in the transactions input array. The SDSTransaction instances returned in the transactions array have a name attribute value of SDSTransaction and type attribute value of SDS_TransactionType (4). The transaction dynamic attributes are listed in the following table.
member.returns:
creates and populates an SDSTransaction dynamic object for each active transaction
Name (JOML Type)Description
startTime (TimeStamp)Time at which the transaction started
status (Integer)Transaction status (For possible values, see enumeration)
statusText (String)Descriptive text that is displayed for the transaction status
transactionID (Decimal)Identifier of the transaction
userName (String)Name of the user

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsGetTransactionsAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsGetTransactionsAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, creates and populates an SDSTransaction dynamic object for each active transaction that is currently being replayed or isolated on the secondary database system specified in the secondaryName parameter and returns these transaction dynamic objects in the transactions input array. For details about the transaction object values, see the SdsGetTransactions method. The caller is responsible for deletion of these transient dynamic objects. Deletion is best achieved by purging the array when the entries have been processed.
member.param: Secondary database name
member.returns: Each active transaction that is currently being replayed or isolated on the secondary database system

An array of JoobDynamicObjects detailing active transactions that is currently being replayed or isolated on the secondary database system, (See for a description of what information the JoobDynamicObject contains)

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsInitiateHostileTakeover
member.summary: The SdsInitiateHostileTakeover method of the DatabaseAdmin class initiates a hostile take-over of the primary database by the executing secondary system without involving the primary system. This method should be invoked only within an SDS secondary system. Invoking this method from within a primary system raises an exception.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsInitiateTakeover(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.SDSTakeoverState,System.String)
member.summary: The SdsInitiateTakeover method of the DatabaseAdmin class initiates a negotiated take-over of the primary database by the secondary server specified in the nextPrimaryServer parameter. The takeoverMode parameter specifies how the take-over operation will deal with potential conflicts between executing reader sessions and database state that is being isolated, which will become visible when the secondary server assumes the role of a primary database. This method should be invoked only within an SDS primary system. Invoking this method from within a secondary system raises an exception.
member.param: how the take-over operation will deal with potential conflicts between executing reader sessions and database
Primary database name

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsIsInitialized
member.summary: The SdsIsInitialized method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid at the primary and secondary systems, returns true if the Synchronized Database Service (SDS) environment is initialized for the JOOB system.
member.returns: If SDS is not initialized or the JOOB system is not SDS-capable, this method returns false, otherwise true.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsIsRunning
member.summary: The SdsIsRunning method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid at the primary and secondary systems, returns true if SDS is running (that is, initialized and active) on the JOOB system. On a primary database, you can stop the SDS service by calling the sdsStopService method at the primary database server.
member.returns: When the SDS service is stopped, the sdsIsRunning method returns false.

remarks.para: Note Although the SdsIsInitialized method returns false if the system is not SDS-capable, the sdsIsRunning method also takes that into account, so you do not need to call both the sdsIsInitialized and sdsIsRunning methods.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsReconnectNow
member.summary: The SdsReconnectNow method of the DatabaseAdmin class causes the secondary server invoking the method to attempt to reconnect to its configured primary server rather than waiting for the time specified in the ReconnectInterval parameter in the [SyncDbService] section of the JOOB initialization file to expire. You can use this method to attempt to establish a connection to the primary system that was disabled by a SdsDisablePrimaryConnection or SdsDisablePrimaryConnectionAt method call; for example, re-establishing a disrupted network link caused by a problem in the network path.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsReplayNextJournal
member.summary: The SdsReplayNextJournal method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the secondary system, initiates a replay of the next ready journal on a secondary database when journal replay is disabled. If you want to initiate the replaying of the next ready journal on the secondary database server node when tracking is enabled (for example, when tracking was halted due to a missing journal), use the sdsResume method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsReplayNextJournalAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsReplayNextJournalAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary system, initiates a replay of the next ready journal on the secondary database specified in the secondaryName parameter when journal replay is disabled. If you want to initiate the replaying of the next ready journal on the secondary database server node when tracking is enabled (for example, when tracking was halted due to a missing journal), use the SdsResumeAt method.
member.param: Secondary database name

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsResume
member.summary: The SdsResume method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at secondary systems nodes, resumes the automatic replay and shipping of journals to a secondary system when shipping and replay have been temporarily halted due to a missing journal. When journal replay is resumed, the following actions are performed on the secondary server node. 1. Scans its current journal directory for new arrivals and updates the latest ready journal information. 2. Starts replaying from the next replay journal if it is resident on the secondary server. 3. Requests the next required journal from the primary database server, if connected. If you want to initiate the replaying of the next ready journal when tracking is disabled, use the SdsReplayNextJournal method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsResumeAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsResumeAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary system, sends a resume command to the secondary database specified in the secondaryName parameter to resume the automatic replay and shipping of journals to that secondary system node when shipping and replay have been temporarily halted due to a missing journal. When journal replay is resumed, the following actions are performed on the secondary server. 1. Scans its current journal directory for new arrivals and updates the latest ready journal information. 2. Starts replaying from the next replay journal if it is resident on the secondary server. 3. Requests the next required journal from the primary database server, if connected. If you want to initiate the replaying of the next ready journal on the secondary database server node when tracking is disabled, use the SdsReplayNextJournalAt method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStartService
member.summary: The SdsStartService method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database server, starts a synchronized database service on the JOOB system if it is currently stopped. If the service has already started, this request is ignored.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStartTracking
member.summary: The SdsStartTracking method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only on secondary systems, resumes the tracking session on the secondary server on which this method is executed when tracking is inactive (that is, disabled).

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStartTrackingAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsStartTrackingAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary system, resumes the tracking session on the secondary server specified in the parameter when tracking is inactive (that is, disabled).

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStopService
member.summary: The SdsStopService method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database server, closes down the SDS environment on the database server that executes this method, while allowing all other database operations to continue. As this method is not valid on secondary database server nodes, use the SdsDisablePrimaryConnection method to take a secondary server offline.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStopTracking
member.summary: The SdsStopTracking method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at secondary database servers nodes, requests termination of the tracking session on the current secondary server that calls this method. If the tracker session is currently replaying a journal, it continues replaying until it reaches the end of the current replay journal.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsStopTrackingAt(System.String)
member.summary: The SdsStopTrackingAt method of the DatabaseAdmin class, valid only at the primary database system, requests the termination of the tracking session on the secondary system node specified in the secondaryName parameter. If the tracker session is currently replaying a journal, it continues replaying until it reaches the end of the current replay journal.
member.param: Secondary database name

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.SdsTakePrimaryRole
member.summary: Sets the current database into the role of a primary

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseAdmin.VerifyJournal(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: The VerifyJournal method of the DatabaseAdmin class verifies the transaction journal specified in the number parameter and located in the directory specified in the sourceDir parameter. If the value of the sourceDir parameter is null, the current transaction journal directory is used. This method returns the number of errors that were detected when verifying the transaction journal. Details of the verify operation are recorded in the journal-file-name.scan file, located in the default database directory (for example, d:\jade\logs\current\db0000076894.scan).
member.param: Transaction journal number
Current transaction journal number
member.returns: The number of errors that were detected when verifying the transaction journal

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition
member.summary: The DatabaseFilePartition class is the transient class that provides an administrative Application Programming Interface (API) for manipulating and querying the state of database partitions. Use the DatabaseFile class to partition database files and to iterate partitions.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.DatabaseFile
member.summary: The read-only DatabaseFile property of the DatabaseFilePartition class contains a reference to the parent DatabaseFile instance.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.PartitionId
member.summary:
The read-only PartitionId property of the DatabaseFilePartition class contains the partition identifier assigned to the partition.
The value uniquely identifies a partition within the set of partitions that make up a partitioned file.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.AllInstances(JadeSoftware.Joob.ObjectArray{JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject},System.Int64)
member.summary:
The AllInstances method of the DatabaseFilePartition class populates the object array specified in
The object array is not cleared before instances are added.
An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.param: receives references to objects stored in the partition associated with the receiver.
Specifies the maximum number of instances that will be added to the object array
member.remarks: Note The allInstances method can used to obtain a list of instances stored in an offline partition without bringing it online.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.BackupFilePartition(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The BackupFilePartition method of the DatabaseFilePartition class initiates a backup of a single partition of a database backupDir parameter. (The backup directory is specified relative to the database server node and must be a valid directory.) This method executes on the database server node, and is implemented and executed by the database engine. The backup process performs various consistency checks similar to a database certify, to ensure the integrity of the backup. Set the verifyChecksums parameter to true if you want checksums verified in the backed up file partition. Checksum verification is performed in a separate pass of the backed up file partition immediately after the copy phase. A checksum analysis of your backed up database partition verifies that the file partition has not been corrupted by a hardware or environmental problem during the backup process. You should perform a separate checksum analysis of any backup that has been moved across media, especially if transferred across a network. Set the compress parameter to true if you want to compress backed up data. You can compress data in a checked or an unchecked backup. Set the overwriteDest parameter to true if you want to allow file partition backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory. When this parameter is false, an exception is raised if an existing file partition is detected. Note Before partitions of a database file can be backed up using the backupFilePartition method, call the beginPartitionedFileBackup method for the corresponding database file instance. Similarly, call the endPartitionedFileBackup method when the required partitions of a database file have been backed up. Separate JOOB sessions can initiate concurrent file partition backups. This allows multiple file partitions to be copied concurrently, which can reduce elapsed backup time when the source and destination volumes are on different physical devices. Caution Because of increased disk contention and disk head movement, concurrent backup operations run slower if the backup is sent to a single disk drive. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file. You can use the JoobDatabaseAdmin class EnableProgressEvents method to optionally notify operation and progress notifications for file partition backups. You must both enable and subscribe to this event if you want file partition backup operation and progress notification.
member.param: backup of a single partition of a database
true if you want checksums verified in the backed up file partition
Set to true if you want to compress backed up data
Set to true if you want to allow file partition backups to overwrite existing files in the destination backup directory

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.CertifyFile
member.summary: The CertifyFile method of the DatabaseFilePartition class initiates an online certification of a single physical database partition; that is, it checks the database integrity. The parent map file must first be converted to read-only. This method returns the number of errors that were detected when certifying the database partition. The CertifyFile method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The number of errors that were detected when certifying the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.Display
member.summary: The Display method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns a string containing details about a single database partition (that is, the name, location, whether the partition is frozen and whether it is marked as absent, and so on).
member.returns: String containing details about a single database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.Freeze
member.summary: The Freeze method of the DatabaseFilePartition class converts a database partition to read-only mode after which all object update, delete, or create operations are not permitted. (See also the thaw method.) Note All objects in a frozen partition are automatically frozen, overriding individual volatility state. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located, there was an error accessing a database partition control file, an attempt was made to access an offline partition, the partition is locked for administrative purposes, the partition is required for object creation, the database is locked for reorganization, or you are attempting the freeze operation when the database is in backup state.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The GetBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the date and time the file partition was stable in a backup where stable means the partition was not updated during the backup and therefore does not require recovery. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The date and time the file partition was stable in a backup

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetCreationTimestamp
member.summary: The getCreationTimestamp method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the date and time the database partition was created. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The date and time the database partition was created

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetFileLength
member.summary: The GetFileLength method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the size of a single physical database partition in bytes as an Integer64 value. This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The size of a single physical database partition in bytes

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetFileStatus
member.summary: The GetFileStatus method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the status of physical database partition. This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine. You can use this method in backup applications to determine the status of database partitions prior to commencing the backup. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The status of physical database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetFreeSpace(System.Int64@)
member.summary: The GetFreeSpace method of the DatabaseFilePartition class evaluates the total amount of free space in a single physical database partition and returns the amount as an Integer64 value. This method returns the number of errors encountered, if any, while performing the evaluation operation. You can initiate the free space evaluation operation while the database is open for read and write access. You must first convert the parent map file to read-only mode. Restrictions for evaluating free space are those that apply when compacting a database partition. This method executes on a persistent server node, and is implemented and executed by the physical database engine. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.param: The total amount of free space in a single physical database partition
member.returns: The number of errors encountered

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetFullBackupTimestamp
member.summary: The FetFullBackupTimestamp method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the date and time the file partition was stable in a full backup where stable means the partition was not updated during the backup and therefore does not require recovery. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The date and time the file partition was stable in a full backup

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetLabel
member.summary: The GetLabel method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns a string containing the label associated with the database partition. When a partition is first created, the label is blank. You can change this label to something meaningful for the application. For details about setting the label, see the setLabel method. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: String containing the label associated with the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetLocation
member.summary: The GetLocation method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns a string containing the file system path assigned to the database partition. For details about setting the location, see the SetLocation method. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: String containing the file system path assigned to the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetModifiedTimestamp
member.summary: The GetModifiedTimestamp method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the date and time the database partition was last updated. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: The date and time the database partition was last updated

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetName
member.summary: The GetName method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns the logical name assigned to the database partition. When a partition is first created, it is assigned a name in the following format. part<partition-ID> The name cannot be changed.
member.returns: The logical name assigned to the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.GetStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary: The GetStatistics method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns statistics relating to read and write operations on the persistent database partition represented by the DatabaseFilePartition instance used as the method receiver. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter. The calling JOOB session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. The logical counts record the number and size of requests that can be serviced in cache, whereas the physical counts record actual disk activity. The returned values include cumulative counters, which are not reset during the lifetime of the database server node. You need to compare values from one execution of the getStatistics method with the previous values, to work out the differences.
member.param: Statistics relating to read and write operations on the persistent database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.IsAbsent
member.summary: The IsAbsent method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns true if the associated database partition has been absent; otherwise it returns false. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: True if the associated database partition has been absent

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.IsFrozen
member.summary: The IsFrozen method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns true if the associated database partition has been frozen; otherwise it returns false. (See also the and methods.) An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: True if the associated database partition has been frozen

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.IsOffline
member.summary: The IsOffline method of the DatabaseFilePartition class returns true if the associated database partition has been marked offline; otherwise it returns false. (See also the and methods.) An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.returns: True if the associated database partition has been marked offline

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.MarkOffline
member.summary: The MarkOffline method of the DatabaseFilePartition class marks a database partition as officially absent so that it can be taken offline. This enables you to distinguish attempted accesses to objects in the partition from accesses to objects in a missing partition. When a non-frozen partition is marked offline, transactions are blocked, a database quiet point is acquired, and the file is made stable before it is marked offline. Transaction processing is re-enabled once the operation has completed or aborted. A database quiet point is required to ensure there are no updates to the partition in the pipeline that would require undoing updates to the partition should a transaction abort after it has been marked offline. You cannot mark a partition offline that is required for object creation (that is, any partition in the creation window). An exception is raised if the database file or partition is not created, the partition is required for object creation, the database file is locked for reorganization, or you are attempting the operation when the database is in backup state.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.MarkOnline
member.summary: The MarkOnline method of the DatabaseFilePartition class marks a database partition as present after it has been brought back online. An exception is raised if there was an error accessing a database partition control file, the database file is locked for reorganization, or the database file being opened is required but was not found.
member.remarks: Note Operator actions may be required to ensure that partitions stored on archival media are actually online.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.Move(System.String)
member.summary: The Move method of the DatabaseFilePartition class changes the location attribute and moves the database partition using intrinsic database backup routines to the location specified by the destination parameter. The database path is relative to the database server. If the destination of the move is on the same physical device as the source, the move is accomplished via a file system move or rename action. In this scenario, access to the file is blocked for the duration of the rename and metadata updates. If the destination is on a different physical device, the move is accomplished using a backup copy and verify, followed by a remove of the source file. When a non-frozen partition is moved to a different physical device, the move partition operation blocks new transactions, acquires a database quiet point, and freezes the partition for the duration of the operation; transactions are unblocked after the partition has transitioned to a frozen state. The quiet point is required to ensure there are no updates to the partition in the pipeline that would require undo should a transaction abort. You cannot move a partition required for object creation (that is, any partition in the creation window). An exception is raised if there was an error accessing a database partition control file, the database file was not located, the database file is locked for reorganization or administrative purposes, you are attempting the operation on an offline partition or a partition that is required for object creation, or you are attempting the operation when the database is in backup state.
member.param: The new location for the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.SetLabel(System.String)
member.summary: The SetLabel method of the DatabaseFilePartition class changes the logical label associated with the database partition. This will not affect the external file name and can be changed at any time. An exception is raised if the database partition was not located or there was an error accessing a database partition control file.
member.param: The logical label associated with the database partition

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.SetLocation(System.String)
member.summary: The SetLocation method of the DatabaseFilePartition class changes the default or designated physical location of a database partition in its control record. The location parameter specifies the file system directory in which the offline partition will be located when it is marked online. The value of the location attribute is stored in the partition control file and is similar in function to the path attribute for map files, which is stored in the global database control file. This method causes the database to open the file in that location and requires that someone or something has put the file in that location before it is marked online. A typical usage for this would be to allow offline partitions that have been burned to optical media to be mounted in a different file system directory (for example, a different drive on Windows). In order to bring such a partition online, a tool or application would call the setLocation method to specify the location and then call the markOnline method, to enable access to the partition. Note Calling the setLocation method alters the location attribute without moving the partition file to the destination. An exception is raised if there was an error accessing a database partition control file, the database file was not located, you are attempting the operation on an online partition or when the database is in backup state.
member.param: Specifies the file system directory in which the offline partition will be located when it is marked online

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartition.Thaw
member.summary: The Thaw method of the DatabaseFilePartition class restores the database partition to its default active state, bringing the volatility of individual objects back into effect, allowing non-frozen objects to be updated or deleted. (See also the method.) An exception is raised if there was an error accessing a database partition control file, the database file was not located, you are attempting the operation on an offline partition or when the database is in backup state, or the database file is locked for reorganization.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.DatabaseFilePartitionArray`1.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockKind.Normal
member.summary: Represents a lock held by a JOOB session. It is released when the session unlocks the object.

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockKind.Local
member.summary: Applies to stable objects and represents a shared, transient duration lock that has an associated node lock (Node) entry in the database server lock tables. There is no individual lock entry for the JOOB session in the database server lock tables (unless the session is a server application).

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockKind.Node
member.summary: Applies to stable objects and represents a shared, transient duration lock that can be held by one or more sessions on the node associated with the node lock. The associated background session of the node is used as the locking session. A node lock is released when an exclusive lock request is received or the object is removed from the node’s cache and there are no sessions on the associated node that have the object locked with local locks.

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock
member.summary: Instances of the Lock class are used to describe the locks and lock requests maintained by the system.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.Duration
member.summary:
The read-only Duration property of the Lock class is set to the value of the duration parameter of the lock request.
The Duration parameter of the lock request specifies if the object is automatically unlocked at the end of transaction time or at the end of the current session (that is, the current thread, or session). If a manual unlock is issued, the object is unlocked only if you are not in transaction or load state. In persistent transaction state, all unlock requests for persistent objects are ignored.
