Similarly, in transient transaction state, all unlock requests for shared transient objects are ignored. A Session lock is therefore not released if the unlock request is made while in transaction state. To release a Session lock, the unlock request must be made while not in transaction state. The values correspond to the Transaction and Session constants in the LockDurations enumeration, respectively.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.ElapsedTime
member.summary:
The read-only ElapsedTime property of the Lock class is set to the time that the lock request has been in place.
For a queued lock entry, this is the time that the request has been waiting. (If you want to obtain the total length of time from the first attempt to obtain the lock up to the time it times out, use the Lock class waitTime property.)

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.Kind
member.summary: The read-only Kind property of the Lock class is set to the kind of node lock for stable objects.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.LockedBy
member.summary: The read-only LockedBy property of the Lock class is a read-only property that is set to the session that currently holds a lock on the target object of the lock. If the lock request is not waiting in the locks queue, this reference is set to null.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.RequestTime
member.summary: The read-only RequestTime property of the Lock class is set to the date and time of the lock request.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.RequestedBy
member.summary: The read-only RequestedBy property of the Lock class is set to the session that submitted the lock request.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.Type
member.summary: The read-only Type property of the Lock class is set to the value of the type parameter of the lock request.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.WaitTime
member.summary: The read-only WaitTime property of the Lock class is set to the timeout parameter of the lock request. This property specifies the total length of time the lock request waits from the time it first attempted to get the lock until a lock exception is reported back if the object is currently locked by another user.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Lock.Target
member.summary: The read-only Target property of the Lock class is used to get a reference to the object that is the target of a lock request.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockArray`1.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo
member.summary:
The LockContentionInfo class is the class that is used to retrieve information about lock contentions. A lock contention occurs when an attempt to lock a persistent object is queued because the object is already locked. Lock contention information is recorded on the database server node. By default, lock contentions are not recorded.
The BeginLockContentionStatistics, ClearLockContentionStatistics, and EndLockContentionStatistics methods defined in the JoobSystem class enable you control the recording of lock contentions. The GetLockContentionStatistics and GetLockContentionInfo methods defined in the JoobSystem class enable you to retrieve recorded lock contention information.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo.MaxWaitTime
member.summary: The MaxWaitTime property of the LockContentionInfo class contains the longest time in milliseconds that any session spent queued waiting to obtain a lock on the object.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo.TotalContentions
member.summary: The TotalContentions property of the LockContentionInfo class contains the number of lock contentions recorded for the object.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo.TotalWaitTime
member.summary: The TotalWaitTime property of the LockContentionInfo class contains the total time in milliseconds that all sessions spent queued waiting to obtain locks on the object.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo.Target
member.summary: The Target method of the LockContentionInfo class returns a reference to the object to which the lock contention information relates.
member.returns: The object to which the lock contention information relates.

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.NodeSDSRole.Role_Replay
member.summary: Replay node role

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.NodeSDSRole.Role_Standard
member.summary: Standard node role

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.NodeSDSRole.Role_Unknown
member.summary: Unknown node role

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode
member.summary: The JoobNode class is the class for which an instance exists for each node in a system. A node is a physical workstation participating in a particular application. A node can be a server node or a client node. One node object exists for each logical workstation connected to the server node workstation. There is one fixed server node and one, none, or many client nodes. A node represents a workstation that hosts the execution of one or several JOOB sessions and it contains a dictionary of the JOOB sessions currently active in the node. A node object is created for each JOOB executable program that is running; that is, a workstation that is running two JOOB applications has two node objects, or logical workstation connections, to the server.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.#ctor(JadeSoftware.Joob.ClassPersistence)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class It also creates an instances in the JOOB database for which this object acts as its proxy.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.Name
member.summary: The Name property of the JoobNode class contains a read-only string of the node name. The computer name, obtained from the operating system, has a unique numeric identifier appended to it.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.OsID
member.summary: The OsID property of the JoobNode class contains the read-only session identifier of the operating system for the node. You should use this property in preference to the less-efficient JoobNode class OsProcessId method.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.JoobSessions
member.summary: The read-only JoobSessions property of the JoobNode class contains a reference to all sessions currently executing in the node. The key of the dictionary is the userCode property of the session. If abnormal terminations have occurred in a node, duplicated sessions can exist. Caution Lock environmental object collections with extreme caution, as this can cause hold-ups when sessions sign off and on and when nodes initiate and terminate; for example, you should never use the foreach instruction to iterate through an environmental object collection. Instead, create a transient clone of the collection and iterate through that.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.JoobSystem
member.summary: The read-only JoobSystem property of the JoobNode class contains a reference to the system object.

member.summary: Get the instance for a specified

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.BeginIndividualRequestsLogging(System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: The BeginIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobNode class starts sampling the individual requests or cache activities, or both, of all JOOB sessions in the local node and invokes the NodeSampleIndividualRequestCallBack or NodeSampleObjectBufferCallBack entry point, or both of these entry points, in the user library specified in the libraryName parameter of the BeginSample method. The NodeSampleIntervalCallBack entry point is invoked once only before these entry points, with the eventType parameter in the entry point set to 1.
member.param: Identifies the sampling context returned by the BeginSample method when sampling for the node started
Logs individual requests to the database of the node
Logs individual requests to remote nodes
Logs activities in the persistent object cache
Logs activities in the transient object cache
Logs activities in the remote transient object cache
Identifies the sample in the corresponding user library invocations
In conjunction with the userNumber parameter, identifies the sample

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.BeginSample(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: The BeginSample method of the JoobNode class opens a new sample context for the node, begins the accumulation of sampling statistics on that node, and invokes the following entry points. 􀂄 NodeSampleInfoCallBack, passing it the initializationParameter string and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 1. 􀂄 NodeSampleNodeInfoCallBack, passing it information about the local node and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 1. 􀂄 NodeSampleProcessInfoCallBack, invoked every time that a JOOB session begins and once for every existing session at the time sampling begins. This method returns the sampling handle number used to identify the sampling context that is opened. All subsequent methods use this sampling context handle as the first parameter. When the BeginSample method is called in your application, request statistics are stored in transient memory for every JOOB session in the node until they are passed to the corresponding entry point in the user library specified in the libraryName parameter. The JOOB-supplied library writes a begin session record (type 6) to the statistics file. If you are using the filesmpl or tcpsmpl JOOB sampling library, you can set the initializationParameter parameter to "null" or to "" so that sample values will not be output. For filesmpl, the values will not be written to a file. For tcpsmpl, the values will not be sent to a TCP/IP connection. Use this option in situations where node sampling needs to be enabled for the JoobSession class GetRequestStatistics method but no file or TCP/IP output is wanted.
member.param: User or JOOB supplied external C++ DLL

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.EndIndividualRequestsLogging(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: The EndIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobNode class terminates the sampling of individual remote requests or cache activities started by the BeginIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobNode class. The NodeSamplIntervalCallBack entry point is invoked with the eventType parameter set to 2.
member.param: Identifies the sampling context returned by the BeginSample method when sampling for the node started
Identifies the sample in the corresponding user library invocations
In conjunction with the userNumber parameter, identifies the sample

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.EndSample(System.Int32)
member.summary: The EndSample method of the JoobNode class terminates the sampling of statistics on the local node for the context identified by the samplingHandle parameter (returned by the BeginSample method when sampling for the node started) and invokes the following entry points. 􀂄 NodeSampleNodeInfoCallBack, passing it information about the local node and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 2. 􀂄 NodeSampleInfoCallBack, which your user library should consider the last call for the node sampling context. The JOOB-supplied library closes and releases the current sampling file, which you can then analyze. You can produce multiple files during a node lifetime, by using the JoobNode class BeginSample and endSample methods, but you cannot sample statistics simultaneously on the same node.
member.param: Identifies the sampling context returned by the BeginSample method when sampling for the node started

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetCacheSizes(System.Int64@,System.Int64@,System.Int64@)
member.summary: The GetCacheSizes method of the JoobNode class retrieves the persistent, transient, and remote transient cache values of the node on which the method is executing. These values, which are in bytes, represent the maximum amount of memory that is allocated by the JOOB Object Manager library for caching objects in the node. See also the setCacheSizes method.
member.param: Size of persistent cache in bytes
Size of transient cache in bytes
Size of remote transient cache in bytes

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetMutexCounts(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The GetMutexCounts method of the JoobNode class retrieves the number of contentions on mutexes used internally by JOOB for the particular node identified as the method receiver. A mutex is a locking mechanism used to ensure thread safety when executing critical sections of code. The contention counts are returned as Integer64 properties of the JoobDynamicObject instance specified by the jdo parameter. The name of each property represents the internal mutex name, and the value represents the number of times that mutex has been contended (that is, the number of times execution of a thread has been temporarily suspended because another thread was executing in a section of code protected by the mutex). The contention counts are cumulative from the time the specified node is initiated. The includeZeroContentions parameter indicates whether mutexes that have not yet encountered any contentions should be included in the information returned. If set to false, only information for those mutexes that have had at least one contention are added to the dynamic object. If set to true, information on all current mutexes is added. The calling JOOB Session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. The GetMutexCounts method clears any existing properties from the JoobDynamicObject instance each time it is called. The number of mutexes reported and the order that the properties are added to the dynamic object can vary from call to call, because mutexes can be dynamically created and deleted. Note If a mutex is deleted then recreated between getMutexCounts calls, the contention count can appear to reduce in value. Any application attempting to calculate contention count differences should take this into account. The mutex contention information is primarily for internal use. High mutex contention counts can indicate bottlenecks that are impacting overall system performance.
member.param: Set to false, only information for those mutexes that have had at least one contention are added to the dynamic object

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetObjectCaches(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.CacheType)
member.summary: The GetObjectCaches method of the JoobNode class retrieves statistics relating to cache activity for the node specified as the method receiver. The cache statistics values are returned as properties of a JoobDynamicObject object. The cumulative counter values are not reset during the lifetime of the database server node, and you need to compare values from one execution of the GetObjectCaches method with previous values to work out the differences. The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integers, which are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: Select type cache type to obtain statistics from
member.returns: Statistics relating to cache activity for the node specified as the method receiver.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetQueuedLocks(System.Int32)
member.summary: The GetQueuedLocks method of the JoobNode class populates the array specified in the locks parameter with transient instances of the lock requests that are waiting for shared transient objects in the node specified as the method receiver to be unlocked by the JOOB sessions that currently have them locked.
member.param: Maximum number of lock instances that are to be included in the array
member.returns: Locks array that is to be populated with the lock request instances

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetRequestStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary: The GetRequestStatistics method of the JoobNode class returns node statistics relating to persistent database requests from the receiving node that is the method receiver. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter. The calling JOOB session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. The node statistics are held on the database server node and relate to persistent object requests received from the node specified as the method receiver. For details about the properties returned in the dynamic object. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls. If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties that do not match the properties to be returned, the existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with the appropriate properties. The method is most efficient when the properties match those to be returned. The cumulative counter values are not reset during the lifetime of the database server node, and you may need to compare values from one execution of the getRequestStats method with previous values to work out the differences. The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integers, which are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: Node statistics relating to persistent database requests from the receiving node that is the method receiver

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.GetRpcServerStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The GetRpcServerStatistics method of the JoobNode class RPC statistics relating to activity between the database server node and the client node represented by the JoobNode instance used as the method receiver. The values returned represent information about the connection to the specified node and totals for requests received and replies sent to it. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter. The calling JOOB session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance.
member.param: Information about the connection to the specified node and totals for requests received and replies sent to it

The user number and text values specified in the userNumber and userText parameters are written in the corresponding records.

The NodeRole method of the JoobNode class returns an enumeration that represents the role of the node with regard to JOOB sessions and the Synchronized Database Service (SDS). An SDS secondary server has two node objects, as follows.
A standard node object to which JOOB sessions initiated on the secondary server are attached
A replay node object to which pseudo-sessions representing JOOB sessions on the primary server are attached

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode.SetCacheSizes(System.Int64@,System.Int64@,System.Int64@)
member.summary: The SetCacheSizes method of the JoobNode class requests the sizes of the persistent, transient, and remote transient cache on the node on which the method is executing to be set to the specified values. The cache size cannot be set lower than the minimum for that type of cache or higher than two-thirds of the physical memory size. In addition, the cache size sometimes cannot be reduced because of current usage of objects on it. If the cache size cannot be set to the requested value, it is increased or reduced as much as possible at that time. No exception is raised. The parameter values are then updated with the actual new cache sizes. Note The values are set for the current JOOB session only. When you next initiate JOOB, the values in the ObjectCacheSizeLimit, TransientCacheSizeLimit, and RemoteTransientCacheSizeLimit parameters in the appropriate [JadeClient] or [JadeServer] section of your JOOB initialization file are those that are used for the persistent, transient, and remote transient cache sizes, respectively.
member.param: New persistent cache size in bytes
New transient cache size in bytes
New remote transient cache size in bytes

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.AdjustObjectCachePriority(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,System.Int32)
member.summary: The AdjustObjectCachePriority method of the JoobSession class changes, through the delta parameter, how long an object, specified by the obj parameter, is to be retained in object cache. If the object is in cache, true is returned to indicate that the retention of the object in cache has been changed. If the object is not in cache or is being updated by another session, false is returned. The value of the delta parameter effectively changes the number of lives in cache of the object specified by the obj parameter. The number of lives for an object in cache is one (1) through 255. A positive value for the delta parameter increases the number of lives up to the upper limit of 255, and a negative value decreases the number of lives to the lower limit of zero (0) lives, at which point the object is removed from cache. When an object in cache is not used for the specified amount of time, it becomes a candidate to be removed from cache. Its number of lives is examined. If it is equal to one (1), the object is removed from cache. If it is greater than one (1), the number of lives is decremented and instead of being removed from cache, the object is treated as if it had just been accessed. This results in it being retained longer in cache, instead of being removed. Conversely, when the number of lives for an object is set to zero (0), it is removed from cache. When an object is removed, its subobjects are also removed, including string large objects (slobs) and binary large objects (blobs) but not exclusive collections, which must be removed separately. The AdjustObjectCachePriority method is a variation of the SetObjectCachePriority method that enables you to adjust the number of lives relative to the current value, rather than specify the exact number of lives. The number of lives an object has applies only while the object is in cache. When an object is first loaded into cache, it is assigned one life only. Lives are not recorded for objects that are not in cache. You can use the AdjustObjectCachePriority method with persistent and transient objects; that is, it applies to persistent and transient object caches. With transient objects, a session can affect only shared transient objects and its own non-shared transient objects. A session must use its own JoobSession instance as the method receiver. Using any other JoobSession instance causes a 1265 exception (Environmental object operation is out of scope for session) to be raised.
member.param: how long an object is to be retained in object cache

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.AllTransientInstances(JadeSoftware.Joob.ObjectArray{JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject},System.Int32)
member.summary: The AllTransientInstances method of the JoobSession class populates the array specified in the objArray parameter with all non-shared transient instances that have been created by the receiver session and not yet deleted. This includes instances of internal classes such as NumberFormat and Printer but does not include shared transient instances. Use this method to check for transient objects that have not been deleted when an application terminates. The maxEntries parameter specifies the maximum number of transient instances. A maxInsts parameter value of zero (0) indicates that there is no maximum number of transient instances. You can use the AllTransientInstances method only a JoobSession instance passed by the current session. If this method is called with a foreign session as the receiver, an exception is raised (that is, a 1265 - Environmental object is out of scope for session).
member.param: All non-shared transient instances that have been created by the receiver session and not yet deleted
The maximum number of transient instances
member.remarks: Notes Do not use the AllTransientInstances method in serverExecution methods or in clientExecution methods called from serverExecution methods. When executed from a serverExecution method, the only instances included in the array will be transient objects that have been created or updated by the serverExecution method. If executed from a clientExecution method called from a serverExecution method, it will not include any transient objects that were created in the serverExecution method and not yet accessed by the clientExecution methods.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.AnalyzeTransientFileUsage
member.summary: The AnalyzeTransientFileUsage method of the JoobSession class returns a string containing a detailed analysis of the transient database file, including counts of objects by class number plus other useful information. Each line of the analysis is delimited by the line feed (Lf) character. Hint An easy way to view this output is to use the writeString method of the File class to write the string returned by the analyzeFileTransientUsage method to a file and then view it with WordPad or another text editor.
member.returns: String containing a detailed analysis of the transient database file

You can call this method only on the session instance of the current session. An exception is raised if you call it on an instance of another session.
member.returns: The number of unprocessed notifications queued for the calling session

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.ExtractRequestStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LocalOrRemote,System.Object)

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.GetBufferStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary: The GetBufferStatistics method of the JoobSession class returns cache-related information about the object specified by the obj parameter. The cache information is returned as attributes inserted into the JoobDynamicObject instance specified by the jdo parameter. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting this instance. Any existing attributes in the JoobDynamicObject instance are cleared when the GetBufferStatistics method is called. The method returns true if the object was in cache at the time of the call, and false if the object was not in cache. It does not load an object into cache if it was not already present. For details about the attributes inserted into the JoobDynamicObject instance. The GetBufferStatistics method can be used with persistent and transient objects. With transient objects, a session can examine only shared transient objects and its own non-shared transient objects. A session can use only its own JoobSession instance as the method receiver. Using any other JoobSession instance causes a 1265 exception (Environmental object operation is out of scope for session) to be raised.
member.param: Object of interest
Cache-related information about the object specified by the parameter

member.summary: The GetDeadlockPriority method of the JoobSession class retrieves the priority value to be used when dealing with deadlocks involving persistent objects. This method can be called only on the session instance of the current session. If you call it on an instance for another session, an exception is raised.
member.returns: The priority value to be used when dealing with deadlocks involving persistent objects

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.GetRequestStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LocalOrRemote)
member.summary: The GetRequestStatistics method of the JoobSession class retrieves node sampling values relating to the current session that is executing the method. The values are returned as properties of a JoobDynamicObject object. You can use this method to directly retrieve node sampling information; for example, if you want to calculate resource usage over the duration of a transaction. The returned values include cumulative counters that are not reset by the method calls. JOOB applications that use this method therefore need to compare values from one call to the next, to work out the value differences. The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes). You should use the GetRequestStatistics method only when node sampling is enabled. If node sampling is not enabled, this method returns zero (0) for all values other than for session ticks. The localOrRemote parameter is set to Local, the statistics for all requests invoked on the local node are returned as properties in the dynamic object specified in the jdo parameter. If the localOrRemote parameter is set to Remote, the statistics for all requests from the local node to remote nodes are returned as properties in the specified dynamic object. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls. If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties but they do not match the properties to be returned, the existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with the appropriate properties. This method is most efficient when the properties match those to be returned. You can call this method only with the current session as the receiver. An exception is raised if you attempt to call this method for a different session.
member.param: Node sampling values relating to the current session
Either Local or Remote

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.GetRpcServerStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject,System.Boolean)
member.summary: The GetRpcServerStatistics method of the JoobSession class retrieves Remote Procedure Call (RPC) statistics relating to requests and replies sent to and from the database server node and the session specified by receiver object. The method receiver can be any current session, including the requesting session itself or a session executing on another node. The values returned represent information about the connection between client node and database server node, and requests and replies between the nodes for the specified session. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. The detailed parameter specifies whether the values include individual totals for each request type. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls. If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties that do not match properties to be returned, existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with appropriate properties. This method is most efficient when properties match those to be returned. The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: Remote Procedure Call (RPC)statistics relating to requests and replies sent to and from the database server node and the processspecified by receiver object
Whether the values include individual totals for each request type

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.ProhibitBeginTransaction(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Set the prohibited parameter in the ProhibitBeginTransaction method of the JoobSession class to true if you do not want the current session to be able to enter persistent transaction state. If transactions are prohibited (or allowed) for a method executing on a client node and a serverExecution method is called, transactions will also be prohibited (or allowed) on the server node. This also applies when method execution switches from the server to the client. To enable the session to enter transaction state, this method must be called with the prohibited parameter set to false. The ProhibitBeginTransaction method returns true only if the current session was already prohibiting entry into persistent transaction state. Note You can call the prohibitBeginTransaction method only for the current session.
member.param: Set to true if you do not want the current session to be able to enter persistent transaction state
member.returns: True only if the current session was already prohibiting entry into persistent transaction state

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.ProhibitPersistentUpdates(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Set the prohibited parameter in the ProhibitPersistentUpdates method of the JoobSession class to true if you do not want persistent objects in the current session to be updated. When the updating of persistent objects is prohibited in the current session, any attempt to do so raises a 1271 exception (that is, An attempt was made to perform an operation that is prohibited in this context). If the updating of persistent objects is prohibited (or allowed) for a method executing on a client node and a serverExecution method is called, the updating of persistent objects will also be prohibited (or allowed) on the server node. This also applies when method execution switches from the server to the client. When the updating of persistent objects in the session of the receiver is prohibited, the following operations are also prohibited. 􀂄 Creating or deleting a persistent object 􀂄 Cloning or copying to a persistent object 􀂄 Executing the beginTransaction instruction 􀂄 Executing the commitTransaction instruction When the updating of persistent objects in the session is prohibited, setting the value of the prohibited parameter to false removes the prohibition and allows persistent objects to once again be updated. The AbortTransaction instruction can still be executed, even if the prohibition is in place. Executing the AbortTransaction instruction removes the prohibition. The ProhibitPersistentUpdates method returns true only if the current session was already prohibiting the updating of persistent objects. Notes This method applies only to the current session. The ProhibitPersistentUpdates method is designed to prevent unexpected updates to persistent objects. It is not intended to be a comprehensive security measure.
member.param: Set to true if you do not want persistent objects in the current session to be updated
member.returns: True only if the current session was already prohibiting the updating of persistent objects

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.ResumeTimers
member.summary: The ResumeTimers method of the JoobSession class resumes all timers suspended for the receiver using the SuspendTimers method.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SendRequestStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LocalOrRemote)
member.summary: The SendRequestStatistics method of the JoobSession class requests a target session to send a notification containing local or remote request statistics. The target session can be any current session, including the requesting session itself or a session executing on another node. The target object for the notification is the JoobSession instance of the session making the request. To request local statistics, which record information about requests made on the node on which the session is running, set the value of the localOrRemote parameter to Local. To request remote statistics, which record information about requests made between the client node and the database server node, set the value of the localOrRemote parameter to Remote. Most of the request statistics are collected only when node sampling is active on the node. The values that are reported independent of node sampling are the thin client statistics that are part of the local request statistics. The target session is temporarily activated or interrupted to send the notification. After sending the notification, it resumes whatever it was previously doing. Note This method is asynchronous; that is, the sendRequestStatistics method does not wait until the statistics have been received. The statistics are received as a notification some time after the SendRequestStatistics method has been called. The session making the request should register to receive type Process_Local_Stats_Event (local request statistics) or Process_Remote_Stats_Event (remote request statistics) notifications on its JoobSession instance using the BeginNotification method defined in the Object class before executing the SendRequestStatistics method, as shown in the following code fragment. self.beginNotification(session, Process_Local_Stats_Event, Response_Continuous, 0); To test whether a notification contains local or remote request statistical information, the userNotification method of its JoobSession instance should test whether the value of the eventType parameter is Process_Local_Stats_Event or Process_Remote_Stats_Event, which indicates a local or remote request statistics notification. The userInfo parameter of the notification should then be passed as a parameter to the extractRequestStatistics method, to extract the local and remote request statistics as attributes in a JoobDynamicObject instance. If the target session (the method receiver) is not a valid current session, an 1128 exception (The target session is not valid) is raised.
member.param: Either Local or Remote

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SendTransientFileAnalysis
member.summary: The SendTransientFileAnalysis method of the JoobSession class requests a target session (the method receiver) to send one or more notifications containing detailed analysis of its transient database file, including counts of objects by class number plus other useful information. The target session can be any current session, including the requesting session itself or a session executing on another node. The information received is the same as that returned by the AnalyzeTransientFileUsage method of the JoobSession class, although that method can be used only for the current session instance. The target session is activated temporarily or interrupted in order to analyze the transient database file and send the notifications, after which it resumes whatever it was doing. Note The SendTransientFileAnalysis method is asynchronous; that is, it does not wait until the information is received. The information is received through notifications some time after the method is called. Register to receive notifications of events of type Process_TDB_Analysis_Event (a global constant in the JadeProcessEvents category) on the session making the request using the beginNotification method defined in the JoobObject class before executing the SendTransientFileAnalysis method, as shown in the following code fragment. self.beginNotification(session, Process_TDB_Analysis_Event, Response_Continuous, 0); To test whether a notification contains transient database file analysis, the userNotification method should test whether the value of the eventType parameter is Process_TDB_Analysis_Event. If the size of the analyzed data collected is greater than the maximum allowed for a notification, the information is broken into parts and sent in a number of notifications. Each delivered notification records positional information at the end of the string, in the format [<session oid>:<current notification>:<total number of notifications>]; for example, [187.5:1:3]). If the target session (the method receiver) is not a valid current session, an 1128 exception (The target session is not valid) is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SendTransientFileInfo
member.summary: The SendTransientFileInfo method of the JoobSession class requests a target session (the method receiver) to send a notification containing information regarding its transient database file. The target session can be any current session, including the requesting session itself or a session executing on another node. The target session is activated temporarily or interrupted in order to retrieve the transient database file information and send the notifications, after which it resumes whatever it was doing. Note This SendTransientFileInfo method is asynchronous; that is, the method does not wait until the information is received. The information is received as a notification some time after the method is called. Register to receive notifications of events of type Process_TDB_Info_Event (a global constant in the JadeProcessEvents category) on the session making the request using the beginNotification method defined in the Object class before executing the SendTransientFileInfo method, as shown in the following code fragment. self.beginNotification(session, Process_TDB_Info_Event, Response_Continuous, 0); To test whether a notification contains transient database file information, the userNotification method should test whether the value of the eventType parameter is Process_TDB_Info_Event. If the target session (the method receiver) is not a valid current session, an 1128 exception (The target session is not valid) is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SetDateTimeDelta(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: The SetDateTimeDelta method of the JoobSession class sets the values used to adjust initial values of the Date, Time, and TimeStamp local variables. The value of deltaDate parameter specifies the number of days by which to adjust the initial value of any Date local variable and the Date component of a TimeStamp local variable. The value of the deltaTime parameter the number of milliseconds by which to adjust the initial value of any Time local variable and the Time component of a TimeStamp local variable. Calling the SetDateTimeDelta method with parameter values of zero (0) causes all local variables to be initialized with the current date and time. If you want to set the date or time to a value in the past, specify a negative value in the appropriate parameter. You can define an adjustment to the initial date or time value for local variables only for the current session. For details about retrieving the values used to adjust the initial date and time used by the receiving session, see the method.
member.param: Number of days to adjust
Number of milliseconds to adjust

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SetObjectCachePriority(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,System.Int32)
member.summary: The SetObjectCachePriority method of the JoobSession class specifies, through the priority parameter, how long an object, specified by the obj parameter, is to be retained in persistent or transient object cache. The greater the value of the priority parameter, the longer an object remains in the object cache. The priority parameter effectively gives an object a number of lives in cache. When an object in cache has not been used for the specified length of time, it becomes a candidate to be removed from cache. Its number of lives is examined. If equal to one (1), the object is removed from cache. If greater than one (1), the number of lives is decremented and instead of being removed from cache, the object is treated as if it had just been accessed. This results in it being retained longer in cache, instead of being removed. Conversely, when the number of lives for an object is set to zero (0), it is removed from cache. The range of values for the priority parameter is zero (0) through 255. A negative value is treated as (0), and a value greater than 255 is treated as 255. The number of lives an object has applies only while the object is in cache. When an object is first loaded into cache, it is assigned one life only. Lives are not recorded for objects that are not in cache. If the value of the priority parameter is greater than zero (0), the SetObjectCachePriority method loads the object into cache if it is not already present, before setting the number of lives. For values greater than one (1), this results in an extension to the length of time the object is retained in cache. If the value of the priority parameter is zero (0), the SetObjectCachePriority method removes the object from cache immediately, if it is currently in cache. If it is not in cache, the method has no effect. When an object is removed, its subobjects are also removed, including string large objects (slobs) and binary large objects (blobs) but not exclusive collections, which must be removed separately, Note An object is not removed from the cache if it is currently being updated by another session (that is, it contains uncommitted updates). You can use the SetObjectCachePriority method with persistent and transient objects; that is, it applies to persistent and transient object caches. With transient objects, a session can only affect shared transient objects and its own non-shared transient objects. A session must use its own JoobSession instance as the method receiver. Using any other JoobSession instance causes a 1265 exception (Environmental object operation is out of scope for session) to be raised.
member.param: Object of interest
how long an object, specified by the parameter, is to be retained in persistent or transient object cache

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SetDeadlockPriority(System.Int32)
member.summary: The SetDeadlockPriority method of the JoobSession class sets the priority value to be used when dealing with deadlocks involving persistent objects. Negative and positive values are allowed. The default deadlock priority is zero (0). In a deadlock situation, the session with the lower value is given the deadlock exception. This method can be called only on the session instance of the current session. If you call it on an instance for another session, an exception is raised. See also the DoubleDeadlockException parameter in the [JadeServer] section of the JOOB initialization file.
member.param: The priority value to be used when dealing with deadlocks involving persistent objects

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.SuspendTimers
member.summary: The SuspendTimers method of the JoobSession class suspends all timers registered for the receiver. Use the ResumeTimers method of the JoobSession class to resume suspended timers.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession.UseUpdateLocks(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The UseUpdateLocks method of the JoobSession class automatically enables update locks on collections. Set the value of the b parameter to true if you want the automatic lock applied when a collection is first updated to be an update lock rather than an exclusive lock. Set the parameter to false if you want to disable the use of the update lock. Note If you do not enable update locks, it is still possible to apply an update lock explicitly to a collection by using the UpdateLock method of the Object class or the lock method specifying Update_Lock for the lockType parameter.
member.param: Set to true if you want the automatic lock applied when a collection is first updated to be an update lock rather than an exclusive lock

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.BeginIndividualRequestsLogging(System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary:
The BeginIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobSystem class starts sampling individual requests or cache activities, or both, of all sessions in each of the remote nodes in the sample definition group and invokes the NodeSampleIndividualRequestCallBack or the NodeSampleObjectBufferCallBack entry point, or both of these entry points, in the user library specified in the libraryName parameter of the BeginSample method.
The NodeSampleIntervalCallBack entry point is invoked once only before these entry points, with the eventType parameter in the entry point set to 1.
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method
Logs individual requests to the database of the node
Logs individual requests to remote nodes
Logs activities in the persistent object cache
Logs activities in the transient object cache
Logs activities in the remote transient object cache
Identifies the sample in the corresponding user library invocations
In conjunction with the userNumber parameter, identifies the sample

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.BeginLockContentionStatistics(System.Int32)
member.summary:
The BeginLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class starts recording lock contentions for persistent objects. A lock contention occurs when an attempt to lock a persistent object is queued because the object is already locked.
After recording has been initiated, you can retrieve lock contention information using the GetLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class. The EndLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class is used to stop the recording of lock contentions.
The value of the tableSize parameter determines the maximum number of individual contended objects that can be recorded. When the first contention for an object is noted, the object is added to the table of contentions, provided the maximum table size has not been reached. If the table has reached the maximum size, contentions for objects not found in the table are grouped together in a single entry identified by a null object identifier; that is, the class number and instance number are both set to zero (0).
As a guideline, the size of each entry is approximately 40 bytes, so 25,000 entries would consume approximately 1M byte of memory.
Only one session at a time can control the recording of lock contentions. If a session executes the BeginLockContentionStatistics method when recording of lock contentions has already been initiated by another session, an 1131 exception (Another session is currently in control of lock contention statistics) is raised. However, sessions other than the one that initiated lock contention recording can retrieve lock contention information, but you should be aware that the information may be cleared or become unavailable at any time.
If the session that started to record lock contentions terminates without having called the EndLockContentionStatistics method to stop lock contention recording, the lock contention recording is automatically ended.
member.param: The value determines the maximum number of individual contended objects that can be recorded.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.BeginObjectTracking(System.String)
member.summary:
The BeginObjectTracking method of the JoobSystem class starts recording, in a file (on the database server node) specified by the fileName parameter, every persistent object read operation from the database or write operation to the database. The information recorded distinguishes between read operations to get an object and read operations to lock an object. For write operations, it also distinguishes between write operations to create an object, write operations to delete an object, and write operations to update an object.
Note This does not necessarily record every time an application uses an object, because if the object resides in the persistent object cache, it may not have to be fetched from the database.
Object tracking is terminated by using the EndObjectTracking method defined in the JoobSystem class.
The file is a standard text file. The first line is a header record and the last line is a trailer record. The lines in between represent individual object accesses. In each line, fields are separated by spaces.
The format of the three types of records is similar to that used for node sampling files.
Only one object tracking session can be active at a time. If the BeginObjectTracking method is called when object tracking is already active, an 1138 exception (Object tracking is already active) is raised.
member.param: The file (on the database server node) where tracking data is written
member.remarks: Caution Use this method with caution. When object tracking is active, the tracking file can fill very rapidly. Object tracking should therefore be used in relatively short bursts.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.BeginSample(System.Int32,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
The BeginSample method of the JoobSystem class opens a new sampling context for each of the nodes in the sample definition group, begins the accumulation of sampling statistics for those nodes, and invokes the following entry points.
􀂄 NodeSampleInfoCallBack, passing it the initializationParameter string and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 1.
􀂄 NodeSampleNodeInfoCallBack, passing it information about the local node and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 1.
􀂄 NodeSampleProcessInfoCallBack, invoked every time a session begins and once for every existing session at the time sampling begins.
The samplingHandle parameter identifies the context that is sampled. (This is the identifier of the sampling context, returned by the BeginSampleGroupDefinition method when sampling started.)
When this method is called in your application, request statistics are stored in transient memory for every session in the nodes in the group until they are passed to the corresponding entry point in the user library specified in the libraryName parameter.
If you are using the filesmpl or tcpsmpl Joob sampling library, you can set the initializationParameter parameter to "null" or to "" so that sample values will not be output. For filesmpl, the values will not be written to a file. For tcpsmpl, the values will not be sent to a TCP/IP connection. Use this option in situations where node sampling needs to be enabled for the JoobSession class GetRequestStatistics method but no file or TCP/IP output is wanted.
The Joob-supplied library writes a begin session record (type 6) to the statistics file.
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method
User library specified that handles C++ sampling call-backs
User defined value

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.BeginSampleGroupDefinition
member.summary:
The BeginSampleGroupDefinition method of the JoobSystem class opens a new remote sampling context for a group of nodes. The nodes are included in the sampling context by using the JoobSystem class EnableRemoteSampling method.
The BeginSampleGroupDefinition method returns the sampling handle number used to identify the sampling context that is opened. All subsequent methods use this sampling context handle as the first parameter, and they are initially executed by the server node and sent to each of the nodes in the definition group by means of internal notifications.
Any error condition at the individual node level is written to the Joob Object Manager message log file for that node.
member.returns: An opaque sampling handle that identifies the sampling context

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.ClearLockContentionStatistics
member.summary:
The ClearLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class removes all existing lock contention data and restarts recording of lock contentions. A lock contention occurs when an attempt to lock a persistent object is queued or rejected because the object is already locked.
The lock contention table is cleared, but retains the same maximum size. Use this method for recording lock contention activity over set periods without having to end and begin lock contention recording multiple times.
Only the session that started lock contention recording can use this method. If any other session attempts to use this method, an exception is raised.
If lock contention recording is not active when this method is called, it has no effect.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.DisableRemoteSampling(System.Int32,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode)
member.summary:
The DisableRemoteSampling method of the JoobSystem class disables the sampling of statistics on the node specified in the node parameter.
This method takes the specified node out of the sample definition group of the context identified by the samplingHandle parameter. (The sampling handle is the identifier of the sampling context, returned by the BeginSampleGroupDefinition method when sampling started.)
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method
JoobNode to disable sampling

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EnableRemoteSampling(System.Int32,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode)
member.summary:
The EnableRemoteSampling method of the JoobSystem class enables the sampling of statistics on the node specified in the n parameter.
This method includes the node in the sampling group of the context identified in the samplingHandle parameter. (The sampling handle is the identifier of the sampling context, returned by the BeginSampleGroupDefinition method when sampling started.)
Use the JoobSystem class DisableRemoteSampling method to disable the sampling of statistics on the specified node (that is, take the specified node out of the sample definition group of the context identified in the samplingHandle parameter).
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method
JoobNode to enable sampling

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EndIndividualRequestsLogging(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: The EndIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobSystem class terminates the sampling of individual requests or cache activities started by the BeginIndividualRequestsLogging method of the JoobSystem class for each of the nodes in the sample definition group and invokes the NodeSamplIntervalCallBack entry point with the eventType parameter of the entry point set to 2.
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method
Identifies the sample in the corresponding user library invocations
In conjunction with the userNumber parameter, identifies the sample

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EndLockContentionStatistics
member.summary:
The EndLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class stops recording lock contentions for persistent objects and removes all lock contention data.
Only the session that started lock contention recording can use this method. If any other session attempts to use this method, an 1131 exception (another session is currently in control of lock contention statistics) is raised.
If the session that started the recording of lock contentions terminates without having called EndLockContentionStatistics to stop lock contention recording, the lock contention recording is automatically ended.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EndObjectTracking
member.summary:
The EndObjectTracking method of the JoobSystem class ends recording persistent object read operations from the database or write operations to the database.
An object tracking session is started using the beginObjectTracking method defined in the System class. If the EndObjectTracking method is called when object tracking is not active, an 1139 exception (object tracking is not active) is raised.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EndSample(System.Int32)
member.summary:
The EndSample method of the JoobSystem class terminates the sampling of statistics on each of the nodes in the sample definition group for the context identified by the samplingHandle parameter and invokes the following entry points.
􀂄 NodeSampleNodeInfoCallBack, passing it information about the local node and setting the eventType parameter in the user library entry point to 2.
􀂄 NodeSampleInfoCallBack, which your user library should consider the last call for the node sampling context.
The Joob-supplied library closes and releases the current sampling file, which you can then analyze.
You can produce multiple files during a node lifetime, by using the JoobSystem class BeginSample and EndSample methods, but you cannot sample statistics simultaneously on the same node.
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EndSampleGroupDefinition(System.Int32)
member.summary: The EndSampleGroupDefinition method of the JoobSystem class terminates the sampling context identified in the samplingHandle parameter for the group of nodes.
member.param: A returned value from BeginSampleGroupDefinition method

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.ForceOffUser(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobNode,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession)
member.summary: The ForceOffUser method of the JoobSystem class requests the system object to force a sign-off operation for a specified session.
member.param: Specifies the node to which the session belongs
Specifies the session that is to be forced off

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.EnableClassAccessFrequencies(System.Boolean)
member.summary: The EnableClassAccessFrequencies method of the JoobSystem class enables or disables the counting of accesses to classes depending on the value of the enable parameter. If the enable parameter is true, counting of accesses is enabled.
member.returns: Returns true if the counting of accesses is already enabled for the current session and false otherwise. Enabling counting of accesses where it is already enabled for the current session is ignored. Similarly, disabling counting of accesses where it is not enabled for the current session is ignored. The getClassAccessFrequencies method of the JoobSystem class returns access counts for the specified classes provided counting of class accesses is enabled.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetClassAccessFrequencies(JadeSoftware.Joob.IntegerArray,JadeSoftware.Joob.Integer64Array)
member.summary:
The GetClassAccessFrequencies method of the JoobSystem class returns access counts for specified classes. The class numbers of these classes are added to the array specified by the clsNumArray parameter before the method is called. The access counts for classes are held on the database server node, and are incremented every time an instance of that class or one of its sub-objects is written to the database or fetched from the database. Only persistent object accesses are counted.
Notes If the EnableClassAccessFrequencies method of the JoobSession class has not been called to enable the counting of accesses to classes an exception is raised. The access counts do not directly indicate how many times applications have used objects. If an object resides in the persistent object cache, it may not have to be fetched from the database when used. The access counts reflect database activity, rather than application activity.
The access counts are returned as Integer64 values in the freqArray parameter, which is an instance of the Integer64Array class, passed to this method. Each entry in the freqArray array contains information relating to the class number specified by the entry with the same index in the clsNumArray array. If a class number is invalid, the corresponding access count is set to zero (0). The freqArray array is cleared every time the method is called.
The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the two arrays used with this method.
The access counts are cumulative values, which do not get reset during the lifetime of the database server node, are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are added to the freqArray array object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: Class numbers of classes to record access counts against
Access counts for specified classes

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetDatabaseRole
member.summary: The GetDatabaseRole method of the JoobSystem class returns an enumeration that represents the database role of the server node on which the Joob system is running.
member.returns: Enumerated DatabaseRole indicating the current database role
member.remarks: Note The JoobSystem class GetDatabaseRole method is an alias for the DatabaseAdmin class SdsGetDatabaseRole method and it enables you to obtain the current database role for the Joob system in which it is executing without having to create and then delete an instance of the DatabaseAdmin class.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetDatabaseStatistics
member.summary:
The GetDatabaseStatistics method of the JoobSystem class returns statistics relating to persistent database activity. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the returned dynamic object.
The returned values are cumulative Integer64 values representing counts of actions (fetching objects, updating objects, opening files, and so on) that do not get reset during the lifetime of the database server node. Joob applications that use the GetDatabaseStatistics method defined in the JoobSystem class therefore need to compare values from one call to the next, to work out the value differences.
The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.returns: A JoobDynamicObject holding database statistics

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetDatabaseSubrole
member.summary: The GetDatabaseSubrole method of the JoobSystem class returns an enumerated value that represents the database sub-role of the server node on which the Joob system is running.
member.returns: The DatabaseSubRole that the database is currently running

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetDatabaseDiskCacheStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary:
The GetDatabaseDiskCacheStatistics method of the JoobSystem class returns statistics relating to the persistent database cache. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter.
The returned values are Integer64 values representing counts of actions pertaining to the persistent database cache, most of which are cumulative, starting from when the database was opened. Joob applications that use the GetDatabaseDiskCacheStatistics method defined in the JoobSystem class therefore need to compare values from one call to the next, to work out the value differences.
For further explanation of these values, refer to the Joob Monitor knowledge base. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls.
If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties that do not match the properties to be returned, the existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with the appropriate properties. This method is most efficient when the properties match those to be returned.
The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: A JoobDynamicObject that receives the updated database disk cache statistics

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetDatabaseObjectCacheStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary: Method no longer implemented in the database layer, use instead
member.param: Unused

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetEnvironmentServerIdentity(System.String@,System.String@)
member.summary: Retrieve the UUID's from the database server for the environment and server identities
member.param: Environment identity
Server identity

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetLockContentionInfo(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.LockContentionInfo,System.DateTime@)
member.summary:
The GetLockContentionInfo method of the JoobSystem class returns lock contention information for a single object specified by the obj parameter. A lock contention occurs when an attempt to lock a persistent object is queued or rejected because the object is already locked. The information is copied into attributes of the LockContentionInfo instance specified by the lci parameter.
The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the LockContentionInfo instance. The startTime parameter is an output parameter that receives the date and time at which lock contention recording was started or restarted.
If there have been no lock contentions for the specified object, the values of the totalContentions, maxWaitTime, and totalWaitTime attributes are set to zero (0).
If this method is called when lock contentions are not being recorded, the startTime parameter and information in the LockContentionInfo instance are set to zero (0) values. The BeginLockContentionStatistics and EndLockContentionStatistics methods are used to control recording of lock contentions.
member.param: The JoobObject to obtain lock contention details about
Details of the lock contention
The time when the lock contention recording was started or restarted

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetLockContentionStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.ObjectArray{JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject},System.Int32,System.Int32,System.DateTime@)
member.summary:
The GetLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class retrieves lock contention information. A lock contention occurs when an attempt to lock a persistent object is queued or rejected because the object is already locked. The information includes the number of lock contentions for individual objects, and the average and maximum times spent waiting to acquire a lock on each individual object.
Information is returned in transient instances of the LockContentionInfo class, added to the transient ObjectArray instance specified by the oa parameter.
The value of the maxEntries parameter specifies the maximum number of entries to be returned. Returned entries are added to the array in no particular order. When the maxEntries limit is reached, no more entries are added.
The value of the minContentions parameter specifies the minimum number of contentions for entries to be returned. Only entries with contention counts greater than or equal to the specified minimum are returned.
The startTime parameter is an output parameter that is set to the time when the lock contention recording was started or restarted. This enables you to calculate the number of contentions per second.
The calling method is responsible for creating and deleting the transient ObjectArray instance and for deleting LockContentionInfo instances in the array (for example, by using the purge method on the ObjectArray instance before deleting it).
When the method is called, any existing LockContentionInfo instances in the array are not removed. New LockContentionInfo instances are added to the end of the array.
If lock contentions are not being recorded when this method is called, no entries are added to the ObjectArray instance. You can use the QueryLockContentionStatistics method of the JoobSystem class to determine if lock contentions are currently being recorded.
member.param: An ObjectArray of LockContentionInfo objects
Specifies the maximum number of entries to be returned
Specifies the minimum number of contentions for entries to be returned
The time when the lock contention recording was started or restarted
member.remarks: Note A LockContentionInfo instance with a null object reference for the target value indicates that it holds combined information for all contentions that occurred on objects that could not be included in the table because the maximum table size had been reached.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetLocks(System.Int32)
member.summary: The GetLocks method of the JoobSystem class populates the array specified in the locks parameter with transient instances of the current persistent object locks held by all the sessions in the system.
member.param: Maximum number of lock instances that are to be included in the array
member.returns: A LocksArray that is populated with the lock instances

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetMostAccessedClasses(JadeSoftware.Joob.IntegerArray,JadeSoftware.Joob.Integer64Array,System.Int32)
member.summary:
The GetMostAccessedClasses method of the JoobSystem class returns access counts for the classes that have been most frequently accessed since the database server node was initialized.
The access counts for classes are held on the database server node and are incremented every time an instance of that class or one of its sub-objects is written to the database or fetched from the database. Only persistent object accesses are counted.
The information is returned in a pair of arrays. The clsNumArray array contains a set of class numbers, and the freqArray array contains a matching set of access counts. Each entry in the freqArray array corresponds to the entry with the same index in the clsNumArray array.
The entries in the array are sorted in descending order of access count; that is, the class with the highest access count is the first array member, the class with the second highest access count is second, and so on.
The maxWanted parameter specifies the maximum number of entries to be placed in the arrays. The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the clsNumArray array and the freqArray array.
When the GetMostAccessedClasses method is called, the arrays passed as parameters are cleared of all entries.
The access counts are cumulative values, which do not get reset during the lifetime of the database server node, are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values and added to the freqArray array object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: A set of class numbers
A matching set of access counts for each class in the clsNumArray
Specifies the maximum number of entries to be placed in the arrays
member.remarks: Note The access counts do not indicate how many times applications have used objects. If an object resides in the persistent object cache, it may not have to be fetched from the database when used. The access counts reflect database activity, rather than application activity.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetNotes(JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.NotificationArray{JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Notification},System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: The GetNotes method of the JoobSystem class populates the array specified in the notes parameter with transient instances of the current notification requests by all the sessions in the system.
member.param: The notifications array that is to be populated with the notification instances
Whether the notifications to be reported correspond to target transient objects corresponding to this node (true) or to target persistent objects corresponding to all the nodes in the system (false).
The maximum number of notification instances to include in the array
member.remarks: Note As this method creates transient instances of the Notification class, it is the responsibility of the method caller to purge the collection used by the method to delete these transient instances. The collection should be purged before the deletion of the notification array passed to the method in the notes parameter.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetObjectLockSessions(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject,JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSessionDict{JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSession},System.Int32)
member.summary: The GetObjectLockSessions method of the JoobSystem class populates the dictionary specified in the sessions parameter with all sessions that have locks on the object referenced by the lockTarget parameter.
