Specifies the session dictionary that is to be populated with sessions that have locks on the object referenced by the lockTarget parameter
Specifies the maximum number of object lock session instances to include in the dictionary

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetObjectPartitionID(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobObject)
member.summary: The GetObjectPartitionID method of the JoobSystem class returns the identifier of the database file partition in which the object specified in the object parameter is located. (See also the Move method of the JoobObject class.)
member.param: The object for which we are trying to locate its DatabaseFile partition
member.returns: The identifier of the database file partition in which the obj specified is located

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetQueuedLocks(System.Int32)
member.summary:
The GetQueuedLocks method of the JoobSystem class is similar to the GetObjectLockSessions and GetLocks methods, but it includes only the lock requests that are waiting for objects to be unlocked by the sessions that currently have them locked. The value of the maxEntries parameter specifies the maximum number of entries to be inserted into the array specified by the locks parameter.
Entries are inserted in no particular order.
Lock objects returned can have lock entries in the array that have the Lock class lockedBy property set to null if the lock request is still waiting to be processed in the lock queue.
When this occurs, the session that caused the lock request to be queued has already released it but because of high activity on the executing node, the lock request has not been retried.
member.param: Specifies the maximum number of entries to be returned
member.returns: A LockArray with lock requests that are waiting for objects to be unlocked

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetRequestStatistics(JadeSoftware.Joob.JoobDynamicObject)
member.summary:
The GetRequestStatistics method of the JoobSystem class returns system statistics relating to requests carried out by the database server node. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter.
The system statistics are held on the database server node.
The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls.
If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties that do not match the properties to be returned, the existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with the appropriate properties. This method is most efficient when the properties match those to be returned.
The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes).
member.param: System statistics relating to requests carried out by the database server node

member.summary:
The GetRpcServerStatistics method of the JoobSystem class remote procedure calls (RPC) statistics relating to activity between the database server node and all client nodes. The values returned represent information about the connection between client nodes and the database server, and totals for requests received and replies sent. The values are returned as Integer64 properties in the dynamic object specified by the jdo parameter.
The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. The detailed parameter specifies whether the values returned should be combined for all requests, or individual totals for each request type.
The calling session is responsible for creating and deleting the JoobDynamicObject instance. Properties are added to the object when the method is first called. The object can then be used in subsequent calls.
If the dynamic object passed to the method already contains properties that do not match the properties to be returned, the existing dynamic object properties are removed and replaced with the appropriate properties. This method is most efficient when the properties match those to be returned.
The cumulative values are held as 64-bit unsigned integer values, and are copied to the dynamic object as Integer64 values. The maximum value before they wrap around to negative values is therefore 2^63 - 1 (approximately 8 Exabytes)
member.param: Information about the connection between client nodes and the database server, and totals for requests received and replies sent
Whether the values returned should be combined for all requests, or individual totals for each request type

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.SdsAuditEnableSecondaryApps
member.summary:
The SdsAuditEnableSecondaryApps method of the JoobSystem class is intended for execution on a Joob Synchronized Database Service (SDS) primary system. It writes an audit record into the journal that, when replayed on the secondary, starts server applications and enables user sign-on.
It is typically used after a number of schema loads that have changed class definitions but have not required the schema to be versioned. In such cases, applications are stopped on the secondary system to prevent outdated class definitions being used.
Where there is no transition phase to identify the end of the sequence of schema loads, that point can be identified by executing the SdsAuditEnableSecondaryApps method on the primary system.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.JoobSystem.GetProductionMode
member.summary: The GetProductionMode method of the JoobSystem class returns the state of the JOOB database production mode flag. Production mode is designed for use with JOOB systems running in production where changes to the database occur only under managed conditions and performance may be improved at the cost of memory usage. In production mode, internal structures for classes are not released when the class is no longer in use. Subsequent class usages reuse these structures, avoiding the overhead of allocating and opening the class.
member.returns: True if the database is set to production me

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadStyle
member.summary: Controls how the schema is loaded, and the effect the load has on versioning objects. See .

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadStyle.CurrentVersion
member.summary:
The CurrentSchemaVersion value loads the schema into the existing current schema version, which may potentially affect current runtime behavior. As structural changes are not allowed, the load will not proceed if structural changes are to be introduced.
If you are loading a schema file containing only method changes into a multiuser system, select this option, to ensure that no structural changes are attempted that would require a reorganization of the database and impact existing users. If a structural change is detected, the schema load returns an error.
The commands (via a JCF file) that can be performed only in the current schema version are
Create Database File
Delete Database File
Modify Schema
Move Instances
Remap Class
Delete Instance
Modify Class
Rename Class

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadStyle.LatestVersion
member.summary: The default LatestSchemaVersion value loads a new schema or loads the schema as the latest schema version (a new version will be created if required), and allows structural changes.

member.name: F:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadStyle.OnlyStructuralVersioning
member.summary: The OnlyStructuralVersioning value loads only structural changes into the latest version and does not version methods or other non-structural entities. This may potentially affect current runtime behavior.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionHelp
member.summary: Display help text.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionServerType
P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDataSource
P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionScmFile
member.summary:
Use the property to specify the fully qualified name of the schema file that you want to load. If you are loading a partial file, this may be a .scm file or a .cls file for a class.
This can also be a multiple schema file (.mul) for loading multiple schemas.
member.remarks: When deleting a user-defined schema (by using the optional OptionDeleteSchema property) or loading a report view, formats, folders, users, definitions, or all report data file (by using the optional OptionReportViewFile, OptionReportFormatsFile, OptionReportFoldersFile, OptionReportUsersFile, OptionReportDefnFile, or OptionReportLoadAllFile properties), you do not have to specify the otherwise mandatory OptionScmFile property. However, you must always specify the OptionDataSource property.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDdbFile
member.summary: Use the property to specify the fully qualified name of the forms (.ddb) file that you want to load, if any.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionJcfFile
member.summary: Use the property to specify the fully qualified name of the JOOB Command (.jcf) file that you want to load, if any.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDisplayTimes
member.summary: Use the optional property to specify the inclusion of current time and elapsed time information in status messages. If then will be set to true

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionNoReorgRecovery
member.summary:
Use the optional property to specify whether temporary backups (.bak files) of the original database files (.dat files) are to be created when a reorganization takes place.
The default value of the property is false, which means that the temporary backup files are created. If you set the value of the property to true, temporary backup files are not created. With this setting, if a reorganization failed, you would need to restore the system from a backup taken before the reorganization.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionFastBuildBTreeCollections
member.summary:
The property specifies whether the building or rebuilding of ObjectSet and MemberKeyDictionary collections is moved from the Relationship Maintenance phase to the Object Conversion phase.
Fast building of collections significantly reduces the elapsed time of large database reorganizations involving collection maintenance by using a faster extract/sort/build algorithm and by allowing the collection maintenance to be performed in parallel by multiple reorganization workers. However, additional disk space up to three times the total size of the collections being built or rebuilt is required for extract and sort files.
Fast building of collections is available only for non-updating reorganizations. If you enable fast building of collections, updates are disallowed when you initiate the reorganization.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDontSaveSources
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify that you want to delete the source code from all JOOB condition methods when the schema is loaded. By default, source code is retained when the schema is loaded.
If you want to release a JOOB environment that does not contain source code, you must perform a full schema load for all schema loads into that environment and specify the OptionDontSaveSources and the OptionCompileUnchangedMethods properties with values of true.
When the methods that are being loaded already exist and the source of those methods has not changed, the methods are not compiled. The source is not updated so it is therefore not deleted.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionProductionMode
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify that you want production mode set or unset for your JOOB database. Production mode retains its current setting by default .
If you specify the production mode other than when loading schemas, you should do so only when loading or following the load of your last schema, as it applies to you entire database in single user mode only.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDeleteIfAbsent
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify whether you want elements (for example, classes and properties) that exist in the JOOB database but are not defined in the incoming schema file to be deleted. You can also use the and parameters to provide you with more-detailed control over the deletion of missing methods and properties.
By default, undefined elements are deleted.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDeleteMethodsIfAbsent
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify whether you want to delete methods that exist in the JOOB database but are not defined in the incoming schema file.
The value of this parameter defaults to the value of the property.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDeletePropertiesIfAbsent
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify whether you want to delete properties that exist in the JOOB database but are not defined in the incoming schema file.
The value of this parameter defaults to the value of the property.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionSuppressWarningDialogs
member.summary:
The optional property specifies whether all warning message dialogs resulting from compiling schema files (for example, class number conflict messages) are suppressed and warning messages are output only to the message log.
This property applies only when loading schemas using the JADE development environment (that is, by using the Load Options dialog).
Set this parameter to true if you do not want compiler warning messages displayed on your desktop when loading schemas.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionClearRestart
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to set this property to true if you want to clear the schema load restart information before loading the schema. When loading a large schema file, recovery information is written at regular intervals to the JOOB database.
If the schema load fails, a subsequent load of the schema automatically restarts the loading of the schema file from the last recovery point. If problems occur when restarting the schema load (for example, the original schema file may no longer be available), the schema may be left in an inconsistent state. You should delete the schema whose load failed, and reload it by using a schema file containing a complete definition of the schema.
You can use this property to clear the restart information so that the new schema file is loaded over the top of the file that terminated before completion. By default, restart information is not cleared before the schema is loaded; that is, the default value for this parameter is false.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionTargetSchema
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify a different target schema name from the names defined in the schema file.
You can specify a new name for your schema or the name of an existing schema if you want to load into a schema other than the one defined in the schema file. This parameter has no effect if you are loading multiple schemas.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionSubschemaOf
member.summary:
If you specify the name of a new schema by using the optional property, you must specify its super-schema by using the property.
This parameter is not required if you specify the name of an existing schema for the property.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDeleteSchema
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify the name of a user-defined schema that you want to delete from a runtime database.
When you delete a schema, all of its class instances are also deleted.
You can remove user-defined schemas only. You cannot remove a system schema or a user-defined schema that has one or more sub-schemas.
To remove a schema that has sub-schemas, you must first remove any sub-schemas of that schema.
member.remarks: As this property is case-sensitive, you must specify the schema name exactly.

You can specify only one user-defined schema for deletion. If you want to remove more than one schema (for example, sub-schemas and then their super-schema), you must run and instance JoobLoader multiple times. In addition, you cannot delete a schema in JoobLoader execution that loads a schema (that is, you must specify either the property or the property, but not both), a user-defined schema that is in reorganization state.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionUnversionAllSchemas
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify that you want to remove the latest version of all versioned schemas, by setting this parameter to true.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionUnversionSchema
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify that you want to remove the latest version of a versioned schema (and any related schema) specified in the property when a new version is loaded, by setting this parameter to true.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionLoadStyle
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the load style. See for valid values, and associated behaviour.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionIgnoreEmptyMethods
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to override the default behavior when an empty method implementation is found while loading a schema file. The default behavior removes the existing source for the method and marks it as needing compilation (that is, if you do not specify the property, it is assumed to be false).
Set this property to true when you want to leave the existing source and code of a method with an empty implementation untouched during a schema load.
The expected use of this property occurs when an extracted schema that includes methods that had their source stripped is loaded into an environment where those methods had previously been loaded from an encrypted schema.
member.remarks: Overriding the default behavior by using the property is considered potentially unsafe.

summary.para: The optional property enables you to specify whether you want all methods loaded from the schema file to be compiled during the load process, including those whose source is the same as their source in the database.
By default, the load process skips methods whose source is the same as their source in the database (that is, the default value is false).

If you want to release a JOOB environment that does not contain source code, you must perform a full schema load for all schema loads into that environment and specify the and properties with values of true.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionOverridePatchVersion
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to override the patch version number in schema entities by specifying that all loaded entities have their patch version number set to the specified value, which can be in the range 1 through Max_Integer - 1 (that is, 2,147,483,646).
When you specify a valid value, it is effective regardless of whether patch versioning is enabled for the schema. A value of zero (0) causes the property to be ignored. The property is not required if you want to retain the patch number specified in the schema file.
The specified value does not have to match an existing defined patch number. If the value is the same as an existing patch number, the state of the existing patch version is ignored (that is, the existing patch number in the schema file may be closed).

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionReplayableReorg
member.summary:
If the EnableArchivalRecovery parameter in the [PersistentDb] section of the JOOB configuration file is set to true, in the optional property you can specify:
trueif you want to perform a replayable reorganization of your JOOB database when the schema is loaded
falseif you do not want to perform a replayable reorganization of your JOOB database when the schema is loaded
defaultif you want to perform a replayable reorganization only if the schemas are loaded into a primary database

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionSuppressReorg
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify if you want to prevent the reorganization of any loaded schemas that require reorganization on completion of the load process.
By default, any loaded schemas that require reorganization on completion of the load process are reorganized, regardless of whether they required reorganization prior to the load; that is, the default value is false.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionShowProgress
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify that you want to suppress the progress (status) message display resulting from compiling schema files (for example, class number conflict messages) and from any reorganization, by setting this parameter to false.
When using a command file to create, rename, move, or delete entities during the batch loading of forms and schemas, all versioned schemas are reorganized by default at the end of a successful command file load (unless the property is set to true).
Suppressing progress dialogs is useful when loading from a script or command file.
By default, progress messages are displayed; that is, the default value is true.
member.remarks: When a transaction is committed (that is, no errors are found) and reorganization is not suppressed, a scan is made for schemas requiring reorganization. If any schema is found to require reorganization, all schemas are reorganized.
Full details of the load operation are still output to the appropriate log file.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionWaitForReorg
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify that you do not want to wait for a reorganization to complete, by setting the value to false.
As the load process in single user mode must wait for any reorganization to complete, this parameter is set to true by default if the value of the property is "singleUser".
The waitForReorg parameter is set to true by default.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionReorgAllowUpdates
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify whether you want to allow other users to continue development and update the database while the reorganization is in progress when you are loading schemas in multiuser mode.
In multiuser mode, the property is set to true by default, to allow updates to proceed before the transition is initiated; for example, other users can still access the database.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionInitiateTransition
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to override the default behavior when the transition phase of a reorganization is reached.
By default, the transition phase of the reorganization is attempted as soon as the object conversion phase is complete.
Set this property to false if you want to suspend the reorganization and explicitly initiate the transition at a later stage.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionDelta
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify the delta into which loaded methods will be checked out. You must also specify a value in the property.
When you specify the and properties and the specified delta does not exist, the load process fails if you have not set the property to true.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionCreateDeltaIfMissing
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify whether you want a delta created if you specify in the delta parameter the name of a delta that does not exist. The default value of this property is false; that is, the delta is not created and the load process fails if you no do not set this property to true and you specify values in the and properties.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionUserName
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to specify the user name to associate with the delta. You must also specify a value in the property.
When you specify the and properties and the specified delta does not exist, the load process fails if you have not set the property to true.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.IsMultiUser
member.summary: Allow checking it the instance has connected to the database in multi-user or single-user mode

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.IsBusy
member.summary: Provides information indicating if the JoobLoader instance is currently busy

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.CompletedWithErrors
member.summary: Once load (and optionally reorganization) operation has completed, this property indicates if the property indicates an error condition occurred or not.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.ExitCode
member.summary: Once load (and optionally reorganization) operation has completed, this property indicates the JOM error code that the operation completed with.

member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a JOOB Report Writer report definitions unload (extract) file that you want to load.

member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a JOOB Report Writer reporting view unload (extract) file that you want to load.

member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a JOOB Report Writer report users unload (extract) file that you want to load.

member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a JOOB Report Writer report folders unload (extract) file that you want to load.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionReportFormatsFile
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a JOOB Report Writer formats unload (extract) file that you want to load.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionReportLoadAllFile
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify the fully qualified name of a single unload (extract) file that contains all JOOB Report Writer view, folder, system option, user, and report definitions that you want to load.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionReportReplaceView
member.summary: The optional property enables you to specify whether the JOOB Report Writer view file being loaded should replace any existing view with the same name.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionSchema
member.summary: The optional property allows you to specify an alternate schema.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionApp
member.summary: The optional property allows you to specify an alternate application name.

member.name: P:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.OptionAllowCircularPackages
member.summary:
The optional property enables you to set this property to true if you want to allow a circular dependency between packages in the schema hierarchy; that is, to permit the loading of an incomplete package (for example, Schema1 exports Package1 and imports Package2, while Schema2 exports Package2 and imports Package1). When you subsequently create a package that would result in circularities, you are prompted to confirm that you want to continue and allow a circular dependency between packages in the schema hierarchy.
The default value is false; that is, the packages that are available for import are those that would not result in circular dependencies in the schema hierarchy.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.DisplayHelp
member.summary: Event occurs when user selection options requests help text to be displayed

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.StatusMessage
member.summary: Event occurs when general status messages are available to update the user on progress

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.StatusJcfMessage
member.summary: Event occurs when general status messages are available to update the user on progress

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Event occurs when a problem has been encountered, this could be when setting or validating user options, loading and compiling the schema, or performing the database reorganization.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.MethodInError
member.summary: This event occurs when a specific error occurs, which is when a method (or condition) is being processed and is invalid.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.SchemaNeedsReorganization
member.summary: This events is an informational event indicating that a schema has introduced structural changes and a reorganization is required.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.ProcessBlockingReorganization
member.summary: This event occurs when a reorganization is being performed and the reorganization task can not continue because the specified JoobSession is using (has opened) the classes that the reorganization task needs to mutate.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.UpdatePercentage
member.summary: This informational event provides percentage feedback information on the progress of the active task.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.UpdateProgress
member.summary: This informational event provides more detailed feedback information on the operation being performed, the current target of the operation, and an indication of the progress.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.MessageBox
member.summary: This event is for message box style provision of information, and soliciting a response from the user

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.DeleteIfAbsentConfirmation
member.summary: This event is for when the schema load process finds entities in the currently loaded schema and they not present in the incoming schema. The missing entity is named, and a user response is solicited requesting permission to delete the old entity. Event enabled if , , or properties are set to DeleteIfAbsent.Confirm.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadSchemaFileFailed
member.summary: This error event occurs when an error is encountered loading the schema file. The error is often related to some form of file corruption or not being able to be read.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.DisplayCompilerError
member.summary: This error event occurs when the schema compile task had an error or finds a situation that is not supported.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.ReorganizationComplete
member.summary: This informational event occurs when a reorganization has completed. It provides information indicating if the reorganization was successful, completed but with warnings, or there was an error.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.JcfComplete
member.summary: This informational event occurs when the JOOB Command File (JCF) processing has completed. It provides information indicating if the command file handling was successful, completed but with warnings, or there was an error.

member.name: E:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.LoadComplete
member.summary: This informational event occurs when the whole schema load process has completed. It provides information indicating if the while load operation was successful, completed but with warnings, or there was an error.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.VerifyOptions
member.summary: Once all the Option properties have been set as required. This method should be called to verify that there is no incompatibility between the option settings.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.Start
member.summary: Initiate the schema load, and optionally the database reorganization. This operation is asynchronous, the operations are performed on a separate thread.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.Start(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Initiate the schema load, and optionally the database reorganization. Blocks the current thread until the load (and optionally database reorganization) finishes, or the expires.
member.param: Provides a maximum time interval to wait; TimeSpan.Zero indicates do not wait, TimeSpan.MaxValue indicates wait forever
member.returns: true if the current operation completes; otherwise, false indicating the time interval was exceeded.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.Wait(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Blocks the current thread until the load (and optionally database reorganization) finishes, or the expires.
member.param: Provides a maximum time interval to wait; TimeSpan.Zero indicates do not wait, TimeSpan.MaxValue indicates wait forever
member.returns: true if the current operation completes; otherwise, false indicating the time interval was exceeded.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.Wait
member.summary: Block the current thread until the load (and optionally database reorganization) finishes.
member.returns: true if the current operation completes; otherwise, false indicating the time interval was exceeded.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.Stop
member.summary: Request the in progress schema load to terminate.

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.Management.Tools.JoobLoader.AbortReorg
member.summary: If the reorganization is in progress, this method allows it to be aborted.

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile_Management
member.summary: DatabaseFile_Management - extension methods for DatabaseFile Created to avoid dragging references to DatabaseFilePartition into JadeSoftware.Joob

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile_Management.GetOpenPartitions(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile,System.Int32)
member.summary:
The GetOpenPartitions method of the DatabaseFile class populates an array with references to DatabaseFilePartition instances; one for each open partition of the associated database file. An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
If the value of the maxInstances parameter is non-zero, the array contains an entry for the number of open partitions specified by the maxInstances parameter; otherwise it contains an entry for all open partitions.
member.param: The 'this' used to call this extention method
member.returns: An array of DatabaseFilePartition objects

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile_Management.GetPartition(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile,System.Int64)
member.summary:
The GetPartition method of the DatabaseFile class returns the DatabaseFilePartition instance associated with the partition identifier specified in the partitionId parameter.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
member.param: The 'this' used to call this extention method
Id of desired partition
member.returns: DatabaseFilePartition object with partitionId

member.name: M:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile_Management.GetPartitions(JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.DatabaseFile,System.Int32)
member.summary:
The GetPartitions method of the DatabaseFile class populates the partitionList parameter with DatabaseFilePartition instances; one for each partition of the associated database file. If the value of the maxEntries parameter is non-zero, the array contains an entry for the number of latest partitions specified by the maxEntries parameter; otherwise it contains an entry for all partitions that have not been removed.
An exception is raised if the database file is not partitioned.
member.param: The 'this' used to call this extention method
member.returns: An array of DatabaseFilePartition objects

member.summary: Schema_management - Extension methods for Schema Created to avoid dragging references to JoobSession into JadeSoftware.Joob All of the reorg related methods are contained in this file. Meant that references to static methods such as ReorgInProgress need the qualifier Schema_Management rather than Schema.

member.name: T:JadeSoftware.Joob.MetaSchema.Script
member.summary: The Script class is the superclass of a number of classes that represent schema entities that have source code, which must be compiled successfully for the entity to function correctly. The most obvious example of a schema entity that has source code is a method. However, there are many other entities such as translatable strings and global constants with source code definitions.
